text: file provides guidance Claude Code (claude.ai/code) working code repository.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: landisutils R package helps prepare data , run, post-process LANDIS-II forest-landscape simulations. part -CAST group’s toolchain depends several PredictiveEcology packages (installed GitHub @development branches — see Remotes: DESCRIPTION).

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R package development done via devtools / pkgbuild / pkgload: Testthat runs parallel: true (see Config/testthat/parallel DESCRIPTION). tests/testthat.R sets options(landisutils.cache.path = <tempdir>) suite — individual tests typically create withr::local_tempdir().
code: devtools::load_all()  # load package for interactive use devtools::document()  # regenerate NAMESPACE + man/ pages from roxygen devtools::test()      # run full testthat suite testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-ext_biomass_succession.R")  # run a single test file devtools::check()     # R CMD check (matches the GitHub Actions workflow)

title: CLAUDE.md
text: R files auto-formatted Posit Air save (configured .vscode/settings.json air.toml): 100-col line width, 2-space indent, skip = ["globalVariables"].

title: CLAUDE.md
text: package organized around three R6 classes plus family helper functions. Understanding three objects fastest way orient:

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Base R6 class representing one LANDIS-II extension’s configuration input files. supported extension subclass living R/ext_*.R file (e.g. R/ext_biomass_succession.R, R/ext_dynamic_fire.R, R/ext_original_fire.R, R/ext_social_climate_fire.R, R/ext_forc-succession.R, output extensions ext_output_*.R). subclass follows consistent pattern: 1. initialize() stashes parameters private fields, sets self$path, self$type (one "succession", "disturbance", "" — see .extTypes), primary filename self$files[1]. 2. Active bindings (active = list(...)) validate normalize parameter assignment (e.g. enum checks, .relPath() normalization, yesno() coercion). 3. $write() calls sequence insert*() helpers writeLines() result file.path(self$path, self$files[1]), self$add_file(...) registers auxiliary input files (climate, species, ecoregion, etc.) scenario can collect . adding new extension, mirror existing one closely — scenario assembler relies $type, $files[1], LandisData header set consistently.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Container scenario’s path, list LandisExtension objects, full set input files. scenario() R/scenarios.R top-level constructor: validates inputs, writes scenario .txt via insertSuccessionExtensions() / insertDisturbanceExtensions() / insertOtherExtensions() (partition extensions $type), registers climate/ecoregion/species/extension files returned LandisScenario. $replicate(n) method copies scenario directory <path>_rep01, <path>_rep02, … Monte Carlo runs.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Tracks climate library input files. Built prepClimateConfig(). Climate-data fetching helpers (via climateR, appeears, elevatr) cache getOption("landisutils.cache.path"), .onLoad() (R/zzz.R) initializes .climateCachePath().

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Two complementary families free functions actual text generation data massaging: prep*() functions (e.g. prepInitialCommunities, prepSpeciesData, prepEcoregionParameters, prepFireReductionParameters, prepClimateConfig) take R-side data (often data.table/data.frame upstream LandR / Biomass_borealDataPrep workflows) convert LANDIS-II file formats disk, returning file paths data frames ready extensions. insert*() functions (mostly @keywords internal, R/ext_utils.R, R/utils.R, R/scenarios.R, ext_*.R) return character vectors LANDIS-II-formatted text lines extension’s $write() concatenates. Foundational primitives live R/utils.R: insertLandisData() (writes header + LandisData line), insertValue(), insertFile(), plus yesno() logical → "yes"/"" conversion .relPath() / .checkPath() path handling. generated files start landisutilsHeader() warning auto-generated.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: landis_find() locates LANDIS-II console via LANDIS_CONSOLE env var. landis_run(scenario, rep, landis_console) spawns background R process (callr::r_bg) cds scenario directory runs dotnet <Landis.Console.dll> <scenario_file>, writing stdout/stderr <scenario_path>/log/.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: parser-level harness runs package-generated scenarios official LANDIS-II v8 console validate config-file syntax beyond R CMD check unit tests can catch. Two files: inst/integration-tests/build_scenarios.R — Rscript build_scenarios.R <output-dir>. Downloads upstream Core8 reference inputs (initial communities, ecoregions, species, climate) uses package API (BiomassSuccession$new(), scenario(), LandisClimateConfig, etc.) (re)generate scenario .txt per-extension config files top data. Prints absolute scenario paths stdout (one per line) caller can iterate. inst/integration-tests/run_in_docker.sh — runs dotnet <Landis.Console.dll> <scenario_file> inside ephemeral container LANDIS_IMAGE (default ghcr.io/landis-ii-foundation/landis-ii-v8-release:main). .github/workflows/landis-integration.yaml wires together push main/master every PR; scenario directories uploaded 7-day artifact regardless pass/fail. Extension coverage Docker image — landis-ii-v8-release ships curated subset extensions: registered image (parsing aborts “extension name …”) others parse cleanly need richer fixtures actually run. See comment block build_scenarios.R:162-192 current list rationale, mirror comment omit new extension. adding new ext_*.R extension, also add scenario block build_scenarios.R covering — unless falls one omission categories.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Relative paths config files: paths written LANDIS-II input files must relative scenario/extension directory. Active bindings typically normalize incoming paths via .relPath(value, self$path) — preserve adding fields. data.table globals: new symbol used data.table non-standard evaluation needs listed utils::globalVariables() call R/landisutils-package.R (local call top file, R/ext_forc-succession.R) avoid R CMD check NOTEs. Collate order: DESCRIPTION explicit Collate: field several ext_*.R files use @include ext_utils.R. Running devtools::document() keeps sync — don’t hand-edit. Roxygen templates live man-roxygen/ referenced via @template param_path, @template return_file, etc. Spelling: spelling package used (see tests/spelling.R); add new domain terms inst/WORDLIST rather suppressing check. Extension test references: every ext_*.R extension must corresponding tests/testthat/test-ext_*.R file whose reference values come upstream LANDIS-II repo’s Core8 (v8) test input — .e. testings/Core8-* (testing/Core8-*) directory GitHub. Cite specific path URL ## NOTE: comment top test, prefer values copied verbatim input made-examples. PnET-Succession exception: testings/ directory upstream, test references deploy/examples/biomass-Pnet-succession-example-v8 instead. Config-syntax claims: PDF user guide extension’s src/ parser (e.g. *Parser.cs, MapFileNames.cs) Core8-* test inputs disagree placeholder/keyword/parameter name, trust parser + Core8 — PDFs sometimes stale (e.g. Extension-Output-Wildlife-Habitat v3 PDF documents {wildlife-name} parser Core8 input use {wildlifeName}). Succession-backend coupling: LANDIS-II scenario uses exactly one succession extension, disturbance/output extensions tied specific backend. Root Rot PnET Output (OutputBiomassPnET) consume PnET-specific cohort state (NSC, foliage stack, water balance) work PnET Succession — three always used together stack. Biomass Succession paired Root Rot PnET Output. disturbance/output extensions package succession-agnostic. package enforce ; check assembling scenarios, vignettes, integration tests. Naming: human-readable, suffix helpers (e.g. @family Climate BDA helpers, @family Land Use Plus helpers, @family Biomass Succession helpers). exact string must match across members family splits. Helpers genuinely shared extensions (e.g. suitabilityFile(), prepTopographyFile()) get one @family tag per consuming extension. use @seealso instead: free-form prose cross-extension references don’t belong single family — e.g. R6 class’s curated list shared scenario inputs ([prepClimateConfig()], [prepInitialCommunities()], [prepSpeciesData()]). @rdname caveat: functions share @rdname (e.g. prepGroundSlopeFile / prepUphillAzimuthMap aliased prepTopographyFile.Rd), put @family tag primary function — alias members otherwise duplicate family list inside shared Rd’s \seealso{} block. adding renaming helpers, run devtools::document() regenerate affected .Rd files.

title: Preparing Biomass Succession Inputs
text: sample values Biomass Succession v7.0 test files (LANDIS-II v8): https://github.com/LANDIS-II-Foundation/Extension-Biomass-Succession/blob/master/testings/CoreV8.0-BiomassSuccession7.0/biomass-succession.txt
code: min_rel_b <- tibble::tribble(   ~ShadeClass , ~Eco1 , ~Eco2  , ## TODO adjust colnames   NA_integer_ , "101" , "102"  ,   1L          , "25%" , "25%"  ,   2L          , "45%" , "45%"  ,   3L          , "56%" , "56%"  ,   4L          , "70%" , "70%"  ,   5L          , "90%" , "90% " )  suff_light <- tibble::tribble(   ~class , ~X0 , ~X1 , ~X2  , ~X3  , ~X4  , ~X5  ,   1L     , 1.0 , 0.5 , 0.25 , 0.0  , 0.0  , 0.0  ,   2L     , 1.0 , 1.0 , 0.5  , 0.25 , 0.0  , 0.0  ,   3L     , 1.0 , 1.0 , 1.0  , 0.5  , 0.25 , 0.0  ,   4L     , 1.0 , 1.0 , 1.0  , 1.0  , 0.5  , 0.25 ,   5L     , 0.1 , 0.5 , 1.0  , 1.0  , 1.0  , 1.0 )  erp_df <- tibble::tribble(   ~ecoregion , ~AET ,   "101"      ,  600 ,   "102"      ,  600 )  frp_df <- tibble::tribble(   ~Severity , ~WoodLitterReduct , ~LitterReduct ,   1L        , 0.0               , 0.5           ,   2L        , 0.0               , 0.75          ,   3L        , 0.0               , 1.0 )  hrp_df <- tibble::tribble(   ~Name            , ~WoodLitterReduct , ~LitterReduct , ~CohortWoodRemoval , ~CohortLeafRemoval ,   "MaxAgeClearcut" , 0.5               , 0.15          , 0.8                , 0.0                ,   "PatchCutting"   , 1.0               , 1.0           , 1.0                , 0.0 )

title: Preparing Biomass Succession Inputs
code: ## don't need to function; just need to exist clim_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "biomass-succession_ClimateGenerator.txt") init_comm_files <- c(   file.path(tmp_pth, "biomass-succession_InitialCommunities.csv"),   file.path(tmp_pth, "initial-communities.tif") ) spp_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "SpeciesData.csv") spperd_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "SppEcoregionData.csv") all_files <- c(clim_file, init_comm_files, spp_file, spperd_file) purrr::walk2(.x = rep("", length(all_files)), .y = all_files, .f = writeLines)  ## create the Biomass Succession extension config object ext_biomass_succession <- BiomassSuccession$new(   path = tmp_pth,   Timestep = 10,   SeedingAlgorithm = "WardSeedDispersal",   InitialCommunitiesFiles = init_comm_files,   ClimateConfigFile = clim_file,   CalibrateMode = NULL, ## optional   SpinupCohorts = FALSE, ## optional; v7.1   SpinupMortalityFraction = 0.05, ## v7.1   MinRelativeBiomass = min_rel_b,   SufficientLight = suff_light,   SpeciesDataFile = spp_file,   EcoregionParameters = erp_df,   SpeciesEcoregionDataFile = spperd_file,   FireReductionParameters = frp_df,   HarvestReductionParameters = hrp_df )  ext_biomass_succession$write()

title: Preparing Biomass Succession Inputs
Calibrating the Dynamic Fire System Extension
text: vignette covers calibration LANDIS-II Dynamic Fire System extension – adjusting SeverityCalibrationFactor, per-season FMC HiProp values, per-base-fuel-type IgnProb multipliers simulated fires match observed regional fire statistics. cover empirical fits already done data layer (Mu, Sigma, Max, NumFires, seasonal PropFire) – come NFDB lognormal MLE / count summaries don’t need optimisation loop.

title: Calibrating the Dynamic Fire System Extension
text: calibration built three layers: calibrated, best parameter vector flows back production fire config via apply_calibrated_ignprob() + apply_calibrated_hi_prop() SeverityCalibrationFactor scalar.

title: Calibrating the Dynamic Fire System Extension
text: library project-agnostic expects provide: NFDB-style SpatVectors clipped primary fire regime (optionally secondary, broader regime context) – YEAR SIZE_HA columns points. fuel-type raster covering LANDIS simulation extent, mapping integer codes base fuel types (Conifer, ConiferPlantation, Deciduous, Slash, Open). Use bc_fuel_code_to_base() ’re working BC FUEL_TYPE_CD factor levels. production scenario directory ForCS / Dynamic Fuels / Dynamic Fire configs already written. calibration scenario template built copying patching. spun-IC: produced build_calibration_spinup_scenario() + run_calibration_spinup() – one LANDIS-II run pipeline build.

title: Calibrating the Dynamic Fire System Extension
text: functions designed composed inside project’s targets pipeline (workflow framework). typical wiring; numbered block one tar_target() calls practice.
code: ## 1. Observed fire targets (NFDB-derived; cheap; no LANDIS-II). save_observed_fire_targets(   primary_points   = nfdb_point_fru59,   primary_polys    = nfdb_poly_fru59,   secondary_points = nfdb_point_frt12, ## optional regional context   secondary_polys  = nfdb_poly_frt12,   fire_years       = 1950L:2023L,   fuel_types_rast  = fuel_types_rast,   path             = "outputs/calibration/observed_fire_targets.rds",   primary_label    = "FRU59",   secondary_label  = "FRT12"   ## fuel_code_to_base defaults to bc_fuel_code_to_base(); override if needed )  ## 2. Pre-calibration spinup: one LANDIS-II run with both ForCS spinup flags ON ##    to populate biomass; snapshot via the Output Biomass Community extension. ##    The snapshot's year-0 CSV+TIF become the calibration IC. build_calibration_spinup_scenario(   out_dir      = "LANDIS-II/_calibration_spinup",   template_dir = "LANDIS-II/phase_2_ICH",          ## any non-fire scenario will do   duration     = 1L,   cell_length  = 100L ) spinup_csv <- run_calibration_spinup(   scenario_dir = "LANDIS-II/_calibration_spinup",   base_seed    = 12345L,   method       = "docker" ) spinup_tif <- file.path(dirname(spinup_csv), "output-community-0.tif")  ## 3. Calibration scenario template: copy from a production fire scenario, swap ##    in the spinup IC, patch ForCS to skip succession, write a fresh baseline ##    dynamic-fire.txt from the supplied tables. build_calibration_scenario_template(   out_dir                    = "LANDIS-II/_calibration",   template_dir               = "LANDIS-II/phase_2_ICH_fire",   snapshot_ic_csv            = spinup_csv,   snapshot_ic_tif            = spinup_tif,   baseline_fire_size_table   = fire_size_table,   baseline_fuel_type_table   = defaultFuelTypeTable(),   baseline_fire_damage_table = defaultFireDamageTable(),   baseline_seasons_sim_table = seasons_sim_table,   sim_years                  = 10L,   cell_length                = 100L )  ## 4. DEoptim driver: starts a warm Docker pool, runs the calibration, tears ##    down on exit. Returns best_params (a named numeric of length 9) + the ##    DEoptim result object + a trace CSV. cfg <- list(   lower = c(     SeverityCalibrationFactor = 0.5,     SpHiProp = 0, SumHiProp = 0, FallHiProp = 0,     IgnProb_Conifer = 0, IgnProb_ConiferPlantation = 0,     IgnProb_Deciduous = 0, IgnProb_Slash = 0, IgnProb_Open = 0   ),   upper = c(     SeverityCalibrationFactor = 2.5,     SpHiProp = 1, SumHiProp = 1, FallHiProp = 1,     IgnProb_Conifer = 1.5, IgnProb_ConiferPlantation = 1.5,     IgnProb_Deciduous = 1.5, IgnProb_Slash = 1.5, IgnProb_Open = 1.5   ),   NP = 60L, itermax = 100L, n_reps = 5L, sim_years = 10L,   weights = c(count = 1, size = 1, area_fuel = 0, severity = 0),   simulator = "landis", method = "docker",   n_cores = max(1L, parallel::detectCores() - 2L), parallel = TRUE,   base_seed = 12345L ) result <- calibrate_dynamic_fire(   observed_targets_path = "outputs/calibration/observed_fire_targets.rds",   scenario_template     = "LANDIS-II/_calibration/scenario.txt",   cfg                   = cfg,   out_dir               = "outputs/calibration" ) print(result$best_params)  ## 5. Apply calibrated params back to the production fire config (e.g., when ##    writing dynamic-fire.txt for the production scenarios). calibrated <- result$best_params production_fuel_type_table <- apply_calibrated_ignprob(   defaultFuelTypeTable(), calibrated ) production_fire_size_table <- apply_calibrated_hi_prop(   fire_size_table, calibrated ) production_severity_factor <- calibrated[["SeverityCalibrationFactor"]]

title: Calibrating the Dynamic Fire System Extension
text: library doesn’t ship target factory (deliberate – target naming conventions vary across consuming projects). Adopt helpers plain function calls inside tar_target(). Three things watch : Cycle avoidance. production tar_target writes dynamic-fire.txt consumes calibrated_fire_params (result calibrate_dynamic_fire()), calibration’s scenario template must transitively depend target. Use build_calibration_scenario_template()’s baseline_* arguments write fresh uncalibrated dynamic-fire.txt inline, depend per-extension file targets populate production scenario directory directly, scenario-assembly target. Deployment mode. calibration target run main process (deployment = "main" targets), crew worker, function internally manages FORK cluster n_cores workers + warm Docker pool n_cores containers. crew worker trying spawn cluster compete cores. DEoptim Suggest. Install via renv::install("DEoptim") running calibration target.

title: Calibrating the Dynamic Fire System Extension
text: sim_landis() per-trial cost dominated LANDIS-II Docker run (~1-3 minutes including dotnet startup). Total wall-clock production calibration: roughly NP * itermax * n_reps * per-trial / n_cores. warm Docker pool (started internally calibrate_dynamic_fire()) saves container-creation overhead dotnet startup, dominant cost remains LANDIS-II simulation . Practical levers calibration takes long: Cut sim_years 5 (instead 10): roughly half per-trial cost. Aggregate fuel_types_rast coarser resolution (e.g., 200 m cells) passing build_calibration_scenario_template(): ~4x speedup. Reduce NP × itermax; DEoptim’s evolutionary search compensates somewhat fewer evaluations. pure-R reimplementation (sim_r_reimpl(), currently stub) remove dotnet bottleneck entirely introduces maintenance burden tracking LANDIS-II’s evolving algorithm. See design notes _tmp_dynamic_fire_calibration.md gitanyow-partial-harvest project.

title: Calibrating the Dynamic Fire System Extension
text: sim_mock() returns plausibly-shaped per-trial output without invoking LANDIS-II, suitable end--end testing DEoptim driver’s control flow: mock varies output par_vec DEoptim sees non-trivial loss surface (good catching driver-wiring regressions). substitute real LANDIS-II simulator – parameter optima sim_mock() meaningful.
code: cfg$simulator <- "mock" cfg$NP <- 6L cfg$itermax <- 2L cfg$parallel <- FALSE calibrate_dynamic_fire(   observed_targets_path = "outputs/calibration/observed_fire_targets.rds",   scenario_template     = "LANDIS-II/_calibration/scenario.txt",   cfg                   = cfg,   out_dir               = tempdir() )

title: Calibrating the Dynamic Fire System Extension
text: vignette("Dynamic-Fire-Fuels", package = "landisutils") – preparing Dynamic Fire Dynamic Fuels extension inputs first place. ?DynamicFire – R6 class backs uncalibrated baseline config writer used inside build_calibration_scenario_template(). ?landis_pool_start – warm Docker pool calibrate_dynamic_fire() starts internally; also usable standalone.

title: Preparing Dynamic Fire and Fuels Inputs
text: sample values Dynamic Fire v4.0 test files (LANDIS-II v8): https://github.com/LANDIS-II-Foundation/Extension-Dynamic-Fire-System/blob/master/testings/Core8-DynamicFire4.0/dynamic-fire_SetUpFire.txt
code: fire_sizes <- tibble::tribble(   ~EcoCode , ~EcoName , ~Mu , ~Sigma , ~Max ,          1 , "fire1"  , 4.0 , 0.1    ,   50 ,          2 , "fire2"  , 4.5 , 0.3    ,   50 ,          3 , "fire3"  , 5.0 , 0.5    ,   50 ,   ) |> dplyr::bind_cols(tibble::tribble(     ~SpFMCLo , ~SpFMCHi , ~SpHiProp , ~SumFMCLo , ~SumFMCHi , ~SumHiProp ,            85 ,      100 ,      0.50 ,        92 ,       120 ,       0.50 ,           85 ,      105 ,      0.70 ,        94 ,       120 ,       0.40 ,           85 ,      110 ,      0.60 ,        90 ,       120 ,       0.70     )) |> dplyr::bind_cols(tibble::tribble(      ~FallFMCLo , ~FallFMCHi , ~FallHiProp , ~OpenFuelIndex , ~NumFires ,             120 ,        120 ,        0.50 ,              2 , 0.5       ,             120 ,        120 ,        0.50 ,              2 , 0.2       ,             120 ,        120 ,        0.50 ,             16 , 0.1      ))  dyn_ecoregion <- prepDynamicEcoregionTable() ## optional  season <- tibble::tribble(   ~Name    , ~LeafStatus , ~PropFire , ~PercentCuring , ~DayLengthProp ,   "Spring" , "LeafOff"   , 0.20      ,              50 , 1.0            ,   "Summer" , "LeafOn"    , 0.50      ,              51 , 1.0            ,   "Fall"   , "LeafOff"   , 0.30      ,             100 , 1.0 )  fuel_type <- defaultFuelTypeTable()  fire_damage <- defaultFireDamageTable()  log_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "fire/dynamic-fire-event-log.csv") sum_log_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "fire/summary-log.csv")  ## these files don't need to be functional, just need to exist ifrm_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "Ecoregions.tif") gs_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "GroundSlope.tif") ua_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "UphillSlope.tif") iwdb_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "dynamic-fire_WeatherData.csv")  all_files <- c(ifrm_file, gs_file, ua_file, iwdb_file) purrr::walk2(.x = rep("", length(all_files)), .y = all_files, .f = writeLines)

title: Preparing Dynamic Fire and Fuels Inputs
text: sample values Dynamic Fuels v4.0 test files (LANDIS-II v8): https://github.com/LANDIS-II-Foundation/Extension-Dynamic-Fire-System/blob/master/testings/Core8-DynamicFire4.0/dynamic-fire_SetUpFuel.txt
code: spp_fuel_coeffs <- tibble::tribble(   ~Species   , ~FuelCoefficient ,   "abiebals" , 1.00             ,   "acerrubr" , 0.50             ,   "acersacc" , 1.00             ,   "betualle" , 1.00             ,   "fraxamer" , 1.00             ,   "piceglau" , 1.00             ,   "pinubank" , 1.00             ,   "pinuresi" , 1.00             ,   "pinustro" , 1.00             ,   "poputrem" , 1.00             ,   "querelli" , 1.00             ,   "querrubr" , 1.00             ,   "thujocci" , 1.00             ,   "tiliamer" , 1.00 )  ## NOTE: `AgeMin` and `AgeMax` specified here (will be converted to range internally) fuel_types <- tibble::tribble(   ~FuelType , ~BaseFuel           , ~AgeMin , ~AgeMax , ~Species                                                                                                         ,           1 , "Conifer"           ,       0 ,     900 , list("thujocci")                                                                                                 ,           2 , "Conifer"           ,       0 ,     500 , list("piceglau", "abiebals")                                                                                     ,           3 , "Conifer"           ,      41 ,     100 , list("pinubank")                                                                                                 ,           4 , "Conifer"           ,       0 ,      40 , list("pinubank")                                                                                                 ,          16 , "Open"              ,       0 ,      20 , list("pinustro", "pinuresi")                                                                                     ,           5 , "Conifer"           ,      20 ,     400 , list("pinustro", "pinuresi")                                                                                     ,           6 , "ConiferPlantation" ,       0 ,     100 , list("piceglau", "-abiebals", "-pinubank", "-pinustro")                                                          ,           8 , "Deciduous"         ,       0 ,     300 , list("acerrubr", "acersacc", "betualle", "fraxamer", "poputrem", "betupapy", "querelli", "querrubr", "tiliamer") )  disturb_conv <- tibble::tribble(   ~Fuel , ~Type , ~Duration       , ~Prescription    ,      14 ,    20 , "WindSeverity3" , "AspenClearcut"  ,      13 ,    20 , "WindSeverity4" , "MaxAgeClearcut" ,      15 ,    20 , "WindSeverity5" , "" )

title: Preparing Dynamic Fire and Fuels Inputs
code: ## create the dynamic fire extension config object ext_dyn_fire <- DynamicFire$new(   path = tmp_pth,   Timestep = 10,   EventSizeType = "size_based",   BuildUpIndex = "yes",   WeatherRandomizer = 0L,   FireSizesTable = fire_sizes,   InitialFireEcoregionsMap = ifrm_file,   DynamicEcoregionTable = dyn_ecoregion, ## optional   GroundSlopeFile = gs_file,   UphillSlopeAzimuthMap = ua_file,   SeasonTable = season,   InitialWeatherDatabase = iwdb_file,   DynamicWeatherTable = NULL, ## not used   FuelTypeTable = fuel_type,   SeverityCalibrationFactor = 1.0,   FireDamageTable = fire_damage,   MapNames = NULL, # use default   LogFile = log_file,   SummaryLogFile = sum_log_file )  ext_dyn_fire$write()

title: Preparing Dynamic Fire and Fuels Inputs
code: ## create the dynamic fuels extension config object ext_dyn_fuel <- DynamicFuels$new(   path = tmp_pth,   Timestep = 10,   SpeciesFuelCoefficients = spp_fuel_coeffs,   HardwoodMaximum = 15L,   DeadFirMaxAge = 15L, ## not needed w/o BDA extension   FuelTypes = fuel_types,   EcoregionTable = data.frame(FuelType = integer(0), Ecoregion = character(0)),   DisturbanceConversionTable = disturb_conv,   MapFileNames = NULL, ## use default   PctConiferFileName = NULL, ## use default   PctDeadFirFileName = NULL ## use default )  ext_dyn_fuel$write()

title: Preparing Forest Carbon Succession (ForCS) Inputs
code: ## write the climate file clim_file <- prepClimateFile(   df = clim_df,   path = tmp_pth,   filename = "ForCS_climate.txt" )  ## write the disturbance matrix file dm_file <- prepDisturbanceMatrixFile(   DisturbFireTransferDOM = disturb_fire_dom,   DisturbOtherTransferDOM = disturb_other_dom,   DisturbFireTransferBiomass = disturb_fire_biomass,   DisturbOtherTransferBiomass = disturb_other_biomass,   path = tmp_pth,   filename = "ForCS_DM.txt" )  ## write the snag file snag_file <- prepSnagFile(   snags,   path = tmp_pth,   filename = "ForCS_snags.txt" )  ## don't need working files, they just need to exist init_comm_files <- c(   file.path(tmp_pth, "initial-communities.txt"),   file.path(tmp_pth, "initial-communities.gis") ) purrr::walk2(.x = c("", ""), .y = init_comm_files, .f = writeLines)  ## create the ForCS extension config object ext_forcs <- ForCS$new(   path = tmp_pth,   Timestep = 1,   SeedingAlgorithm = "WardSeedDispersal",   ForCSClimateFile = clim_file,   InitialCommunitiesFiles = init_comm_files,   DisturbanceMatrixFile = dm_file,   SnagFile = snag_file,   OutputTables = output_tables,   ForCSMapControl = for_cs_map_control,   MapOutputInterval = 1,   SpinUp = spin_up,   AvailableLightBiomass = avail_light_biomass,   LightEstablishmentTable = light_est,   SpeciesParameters = species_params,   DOMPools = dom_pools,   EcoSppDOMParameters = ecosppdom_params,   ForCSProportions = forcs_props,   ANPPTimeSeries = anpp_timeseries,   MaxBiomassTimeSeries = maxb_timeseries,   EstablishProbabilities = est_prob,   RootDynamics = root_dynamics )  ## write the ForCS extension config file ext_forcs$write()

title: Preparing Forest Carbon Succession (ForCS) Inputs
Assembling a Multi-Extension Scenario
text: scenario() validates input files exist disk. demonstration just writeLines("") empty stubs path extensions reference.
code: clim_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "climate.txt") init_comm_files <- c(   file.path(tmp_pth, "initial-communities.csv"),   file.path(tmp_pth, "initial-communities.tif") ) spp_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "SpeciesData.csv") spperd_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "SppEcoregionData.csv") ecoregion_files <- c(file.path(tmp_pth, "ecoregions.txt"), file.path(tmp_pth, "ecoregions.tif")) species_input_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "Core_species_data.txt")  stub_files <- c(   clim_file,   init_comm_files,   spp_file,   spperd_file,   ecoregion_files,   species_input_file ) for (f in stub_files) {   writeLines("", f) }

title: Assembling a Multi-Extension Scenario
text: scenario must specify exactly one succession extension. use BiomassSuccession. Tables match upstream Core8 test input verbatim (https://github.com/LANDIS-II-Foundation/Extension-Biomass-Succession/blob/master/testings/CoreV8.0-BiomassSuccession7.0/biomass-succession.txt).
code: min_rel_b <- tibble::tribble(   ~ShadeClass , ~Eco1 , ~Eco2 ,   NA_integer_ , "101" , "102" ,   1L          , "25%" , "25%" ,   2L          , "45%" , "45%" ,   3L          , "56%" , "56%" ,   4L          , "70%" , "70%" ,   5L          , "90%" , "90%" )  suff_light <- tibble::tribble(   ~class , ~X0 , ~X1 , ~X2  , ~X3  , ~X4  , ~X5  ,   1L     , 1.0 , 0.5 , 0.25 , 0.0  , 0.0  , 0.0  ,   2L     , 1.0 , 1.0 , 0.5  , 0.25 , 0.0  , 0.0  ,   3L     , 1.0 , 1.0 , 1.0  , 0.5  , 0.25 , 0.0  ,   4L     , 1.0 , 1.0 , 1.0  , 1.0  , 0.5  , 0.25 ,   5L     , 0.1 , 0.5 , 1.0  , 1.0  , 1.0  , 1.0 )  erp_df <- tibble::tribble(   ~ecoregion , ~AET ,   "101"      ,  600 ,   "102"      ,  600 )  frp_df <- tibble::tribble(   ~Severity , ~WoodLitterReduct , ~LitterReduct ,   1L        , 0.0               , 0.5           ,   2L        , 0.0               , 0.75          ,   3L        , 0.0               , 1.0 )  hrp_df <- tibble::tribble(   ~Name            , ~WoodLitterReduct , ~LitterReduct , ~CohortWoodRemoval , ~CohortLeafRemoval ,   "MaxAgeClearcut" , 0.5               , 0.15          , 0.8                , 0.0                ,   "PatchCutting"   , 1.0               , 1.0           , 1.0                , 0.0 )  ext_succ <- BiomassSuccession$new(   path = tmp_pth,   Timestep = 10,   SeedingAlgorithm = "WardSeedDispersal",   InitialCommunitiesFiles = init_comm_files,   ClimateConfigFile = clim_file,   SpinupCohorts = FALSE,   SpinupMortalityFraction = 0.05,   MinRelativeBiomass = min_rel_b,   SufficientLight = suff_light,   SpeciesDataFile = spp_file,   EcoregionParameters = erp_df,   SpeciesEcoregionDataFile = spperd_file,   FireReductionParameters = frp_df,   HarvestReductionParameters = hrp_df ) ext_succ$write()

title: Assembling a Multi-Extension Scenario
text: OriginalFire writes FireRegionParametersTable (one row per fire regime), references fire-species CSV initial-fire-regions raster, accepts fuel-curve / wind-curve / fire-damage tables. Values come upstream Core8 test input (https://github.com/LANDIS-II-Foundation/Extension-Base-Fire/blob/master/testings/Core8.0-OriginalFire5.0/original-fire.txt).
code: fire_spp_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "species-fire.csv") fire_regions_map <- file.path(tmp_pth, "fire-regions-map.tif") for (f in c(fire_spp_file, fire_regions_map)) {   writeLines("", f) }  frp_table <- data.frame(   FireRegionName = c("eco1", "eco2"),   MapCode = c(1L, 2L),   MeanSize = c(100, 200),   MinSize = c(4, 6),   MaxSize = c(400, 600),   IgnitionProb = c(0.001, 0.001),   k = c(100L, 50L) )  fuel_curve <- data.frame(   FireRegionName = c("eco1", "eco2"),   S1 = c(10, 5),   S2 = c(20, 15),   S3 = c(50, 20),   S4 = c(70, -1),   S5 = c(120, -1) )  wind_curve <- data.frame(   FireRegionName = c("eco1", "eco2"),   S5 = c(-1, 1),   S4 = c(-1, 5),   S3 = c(1, 15),   S2 = c(10, 20),   S1 = c(20, 30) )  ext_fire <- OriginalFire$new(   path = tmp_pth,   Timestep = 5L,   Species_CSV_File = fire_spp_file,   FireRegionParametersTable = frp_table,   InitialFireRegionsMap = fire_regions_map,   FuelCurveTable = fuel_curve,   WindCurveTable = wind_curve,   FireDamageTable = defaultFireDamageTable() ) ext_fire$write()

title: Assembling a Multi-Extension Scenario
text: EDA wraps number pathogen agents. edaAgent() call emits per-agent eda/<name>.txt file referenced main EDA config. Parameters species rows come upstream Core8 Phytophthora ramorum test agent (https://github.com/LANDIS-II-Foundation/Extension-Base-EDA/blob/master/tests/Core8.0-EDA3.0/P_ramorum.txt).
code: eda_sp <- data.frame(   Species = c("Umbecali", "Lithdens"),   LowAge = c(5, 5),   LowScore = c(3, 2),   MediumAge = c(15, 20),   MediumScore = c(6, 4),   HighAge = c(40, 60),   HighScore = c(10, 7),   VulnLowAge = c(999, 5),   VulnLowMortProb = c(0, 0.14),   VulnMediumAge = c(999, 15),   VulnMediumMortProb = c(0, 0.25),   VulnHighAge = c(999, 30),   VulnHighMortProb = c(0, 0.30),   CFSConifer = c("no", "yes"),   MortalityPlot = c("no", "yes") )  ext_eda <- EDA$new(   path = tmp_pth,   Timestep = 1L,   Agents = list(edaAgent(     name = "ramorum",     SHIMode = "mean",     TransmissionRate = 1.8,     AcquisitionRate = 0.4,     DispersalType = "STATIC",     DispersalKernel = "PowerLaw",     DispersalMaxDist = 1000,     AlphaCoef = 3.55,     EDASpeciesParameters = eda_sp   )) ) ext_eda$write()  ext_eda$files #> [1] "eda.txt"         "eda/ramorum.txt"

title: Assembling a Multi-Extension Scenario
text: LandUsePlus accepts list landUseType() sub-objects, containing one landCoverChange() blocks. three land-use types taken upstream Core8 test input (https://github.com/LANDIS-II-Foundation/Extension-Land-Use-Plus/blob/master/testing/Core8.0-LUC4.0/land-use-change-inputs.txt).
code: ext_lu <- LandUsePlus$new(   path = tmp_pth,   Timestep = 1L,   InputMaps = "landuse-{timestep}.tif",   SiteLog = "output/land-use/site-log.csv",   LandUses = list(     landUseType(       name = "forest",       mapCode = 4L,       allowHarvest = "no",       changes = landCoverChange(type = "NoChange")     ),     landUseType(       name = "FIPSandHarvest",       mapCode = 3L,       allowHarvest = "yes",       changes = list(         landCoverChange(           type = "RemoveTrees",           repeatHarvest = TRUE,           cohorts = list(             cohortSelector(               species = "acerrubr",               ranges = data.frame(low = 1, high = 200, percent = 20)             ),             cohortSelector(               species = "pinustro",               ranges = data.frame(low = 50, high = 300, percent = 30)             )           )         ),         landCoverChange(           type = "InsectDefoliation",           cohorts = cohortSelector(             species = "querrubr",             ranges = data.frame(low = 1, high = 300, percent = 60)           )         )       )     ),     landUseType(       name = "FIPS",       mapCode = 1L,       allowHarvest = "yes",       changes = landCoverChange(         type = "InsectDefoliation",         cohorts = list(           cohortSelector(             species = "querrubr",             ranges = data.frame(low = 1, high = 300, percent = 75)           ),           cohortSelector(             species = "pinustro",             ranges = data.frame(low = c(1, 71), high = c(62, 200), percent = c(20, 25))           )         )       )     )   ) ) ext_lu$write()

title: Assembling a Multi-Extension Scenario
text: Suitability files passed suitabilityFile() sub-objects (plain character vector ’s auto-wrapped). four files mirror upstream Core8 test input (https://github.com/LANDIS-II-Foundation/Extension-Local-Habitat-Suitability-Output/blob/master/test/Core8-LocalHabitat3.0/Habitat_input.txt).
code: ext_lh <- OutputLocalHabitat$new(   path = tmp_pth,   Timestep = 1L,   OutputTimestep = 10L,   MapFileNames = "output/habitat/{HabitatName}-{timestep}.tif",   SuitabilityFiles = c(     "AgeClass_ForestType_example.txt",     "AgeClass_TimeSinceFire_example.txt",     "ForestType_TimeSinceFire_example.txt",     "ForestType_TimeSinceHarvest_example.txt"   ) ) ext_lh$write()

title: Assembling a Multi-Extension Scenario
text: scenario() partitions supplied extensions list type (succession / disturbance / ) writes top-level scenario .txt referencing extension’s primary input file.
code: climate_cfg <- LandisClimateConfig$new(path = tmp_pth) climate_cfg$add_file(basename(clim_file))  scen <- scenario(   name = "multi_ext_demo",   extensions = list(ext_succ, ext_fire, ext_eda, ext_lu, ext_lh),   climate_config = climate_cfg,   path = tmp_pth,   CellLength = 100,   DisturbancesRandomOrder = FALSE,   Duration = 10,   EcoregionsFiles = ecoregion_files,   RandomNumberSeed = 147,   SpeciesInputFile = species_input_file )  scen$path #> /tmp/RtmpseTysi/example_MultiExt_335019ea1723

title: Assembling a Multi-Extension Scenario
code: readLines(file.path(scen$path, "multi_ext_demo.txt")) |> cat(sep = "\n") #> >> generated by `landisutils` (v0.0.48) #> >> do not edit by hand; manual changes to this file may be overwritten #>  #> LandisData  "Scenario" #>  #> Duration    10 #>  #> Species    Core_species_data.txt #>  #> Ecoregions    ecoregions.txt #> EcoregionsMap    ecoregions.tif #>  #> CellLength    100 #>  #> >> Succession Extension    Initialization File #> >> --------------------    ------------------- #>    "Biomass Succession"        biomass-succession.txt #>  #> >> Disturbance Extensions    Initialization File #> >> ----------------------    ------------------- #>    "Original Fire"        original-fire.txt #>    "EDA"        eda.txt #>    "Land Use Change"        land-use.txt #>  #> DisturbancesRandomOrder    no #>  #> >> Other Extensions            Initialization File #> >> ------------------------    ----------------------- #>    "Local Habitat Output"        output-local-habitat.txt #>  #> RandomNumberSeed    147  << optional parameter

title: Assembling a Multi-Extension Scenario
Preparing Social-Climate-Fire Inputs
text: Sample values match upstream Core8 Social Climate Fire 4.0 test input (https://github.com/LANDIS-II-Foundation/Extension-Social-Climate-Fire/blob/master/Testing/Core8-SocialClimateFire4.0/Social-Climate-Fire-Inputs.txt).
code: species_table <- tibble::tribble(   ~SpeciesCode , ~AgeDBH , ~MaximumBarkThickness ,   "PinuJeff"   ,     100 ,                    10 ,   "PinuLamb"   ,     100 ,                    10 ,   "CaloDecu"   ,     100 ,                    10 ,   "AbieConc"   ,     100 ,                    10 ,   "AbieMagn"   ,     100 ,                    10 ,   "PinuCont"   ,     100 ,                    10 ,   "PinuMont"   ,     100 ,                    10 ,   "TsugMert"   ,     100 ,                    10 ,   "PinuAlbi"   ,     100 ,                    10 ,   "PopuTrem"   ,     100 ,                    10 ,   "NonnResp"   ,     100 ,                    10 ,   "NonnSeed"   ,     100 ,                    10 ,   "FixnResp"   ,     100 ,                    10 ,   "FixnSeed"   ,     100 ,                    10 )  species_file <- prepSpeciesData(   df = species_table,   type = "fire",   path = tmp_pth,   filename = "SCRPPLE_Spp_Table.csv" )  ltg_ign_coeffs <- c(-8.5, 0.03) acc_ign_coeffs <- c(-8.5, 0.03)  ltg_ign_zip_coeffs <- c(-8.5, 0.03) acc_ign_zip_coeffs <- c(-8.5, 0.03)  max_spread_area_coeffs <- c(100, 0.5, 0.5)  spread_prob_coeffs <- c(-1.79, 0.06, -0.915, 0.0126)  ## fmt: table site_mortality_coeffs <- c(    0.0059     , 0.00050 , -0.000010 , -0.0002200 ,   -0.00000050 , 0.00000 ,  0.00000 )  cohort_mortality_coeffs <- c(-0.703, -0.9908, 0.009)  ladder_fuel_species <- c("AbieConc", "AbieMagn", "PinuJeff", "PinuCont")  suppression_table <- tibble::tribble(   ~IgnitionType , ~Mapcode , ~Suppress_Category_0 , ~FWI_Break_1 , ~Suppress_Category_1 , ~FWI_Break_2 , ~Suppress_Category_2 ,   "Accidental"  ,        1 ,                   10 ,           20 ,                   20 ,           30 ,                    0 ,   "Accidental"  ,        2 ,                   30 ,           20 ,                   95 ,           30 ,                   10 ,   "Accidental"  ,        3 ,                   95 ,           20 ,                   95 ,           30 ,                   75 ,   "Lightning"   ,        1 ,                   10 ,           20 ,                   20 ,           30 ,                    0 ,   "Lightning"   ,        2 ,                   30 ,           20 ,                   95 ,           30 ,                   10 ,   "Lightning"   ,        3 ,                   95 ,           20 ,                   95 ,           30 ,                   75 ,   "Rx"          ,        1 ,                   10 ,           20 ,                   20 ,           30 ,                    0 ,   "Rx"          ,        2 ,                   30 ,           20 ,                   95 ,           30 ,                   10 ,   "Rx"          ,        3 ,                   95 ,           20 ,                   95 ,           30 ,                   75 )  suppression_file <- prepSuppression_CSV_File(   suppression_table,   path = tmp_pth,   filename = "Example_Suppression_Input.csv" )  deadwood <- tibble::tribble(   ~species   , ~age ,   "PinuJeff" ,   22 ,   "CaloDecu" ,   33 ,   "PinuCont" ,   38 ,   "PinuLamb" ,   27 ,   "AbieMagn" ,   28 ,   "AbieConc" ,   29 )

title: Preparing Social-Climate-Fire Inputs
code: ## don't need working files, they just need to exist acc_ign_map <- file.path(tmp_pth, "Accidental_Ignition_Map.img") ltg_ign_map <- file.path(tmp_pth, "Lightning_Ignition_Map.img") rx_ign_map <- file.path(tmp_pth, "Lightning_Ignition_Map.img")  acc_supp_map <- file.path(tmp_pth, "suppress3.img") ltg_supp_map <- file.path(tmp_pth, "suppress3.img") rx_supp_map <- file.path(tmp_pth, "suppress3.img")  gs_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "GroundSlope.gis") ua_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "UphillSlope.gis") cm_file <- file.path(tmp_pth, "random9.img")  rx_zones <- file.path(tmp_pth, "fire-zones.gis") ## optional  ## fmt: table raster_files <- c(   acc_ign_map  , ltg_ign_map  , rx_ign_map  ,   acc_supp_map , ltg_supp_map , rx_supp_map ,   gs_file      , ua_file      , cm_file     ,   rx_zones ) purrr::walk2(.x = rep("", length(raster_files)), .y = raster_files, .f = writeLines)  ## create the Social-Climate-Fire extension config object ext_social_climate_fire <- SocialClimateFire$new(   path = tmp_pth,   Timestep = 1,   TimeZeroPET = NULL, ## optional   TimeZeroCWD = NULL, ## optional   Species_CSV_File = species_file,    ## ignition maps   AccidentalIgnitionsMap = acc_ign_map,   DynamicAccidentalIgnitionMaps = NULL, ## optional   LightningIgnitionsMap = ltg_ign_map,   DynamicLightningIgnitionMaps = NULL, ## optional   RxIgnitionsMap = rx_ign_map,   DynamicRxIgnitionMaps = NULL, ## optional    ## suppression maps   AccidentalSuppressionMap = acc_supp_map,   LightningSuppressionMap = ltg_supp_map,   RxSuppressionMap = rx_supp_map,   DynamicAccidentalSuppressionMaps = NULL, ## optional    ## topography files   GroundSlopeMap = gs_file,   UphillSlopeAzimuthMap = ua_file,   ClayMap = cm_file,    ## ignition model coefficients   LightningIgnitionsCoeffs = ltg_ign_coeffs,   AccidentalIgnitionsCoeffs = acc_ign_coeffs,   IgnitionDistribution = "ZeroInflatedPoisson",   LightningIgnitionsBinomialCoeffs = ltg_ign_zip_coeffs,   AccidentalIgnitionsBinomialCoeffs = acc_ign_zip_coeffs,   MaximumFineFuels = 500.0,    ## prescribed fire burn window parameters   MaximumRxWindSpeed = 80.0,   MaximumRxFireWeatherIndex = 80.0, ## optional   MinimumRxFireWeatherIndex = 1.0, ## optional   MaximumRxTemperature = 35.0, ## optional   MinimumRxRelativeHumidity = 22.0, ## optional   MaximumRxFireIntensity = 1,   NumberRxAnnualFires = 10,   NumberRxDailyFires = 1,   FirstDayRxFires = 2,   LastDayRxFires = 300,   TargetRxSize = 40,   RxZonesMap = rx_zones, ## optional    MaximumSpreadAreaCoeffs = max_spread_area_coeffs,   SpreadProbabilityCoeffs = spread_prob_coeffs,   SiteMortalityCoeffs = site_mortality_coeffs,   CohortMortalityCoeffs = cohort_mortality_coeffs,    LadderFuelMaxAge = 40, ## TODO   LadderFuelSpeciesList = ladder_fuel_species,   SuppressionMaxWindSpeed = 100,   Suppression_CSV_File = suppression_file,   DeadWoodTable = deadwood )  ## write the Social-Climate-Fire extension config file ext_social_climate_fire$write()

title: Preparing Climate Data for Use With LANDIS-II
code: ## random study area in BC using BEC zones as ecoregions ecoregionPolys <- landisutils::test_ecoregionPolys  plot(ecoregionPolys["PolyID"])

title: Preparing Climate Data for Use With LANDIS-II
code: clim_years <- 2018:2019 ## availability is 1980 to last year  clim_data_path <- withr::local_tempdir("climate_data_")

title: Preparing Climate Data for Use With LANDIS-II
text: Daily weather fetched BioSIM using ClimaticEx_Daily model. high-level wrapper prep_daily_weather() takes study-area polygons object column name identifying ecoregions: Internally, prep_daily_weather() chains four lower-level helpers can call directly need finer control (e.g. schedule per-batch fetches dynamic targets):
code: climvars_daily <- c("prcp", "tmax", "tmin")  daily_weather <- prep_daily_weather(   vars = climvars_daily,   years = clim_years,   studyArea = ecoregionPolys,   id = "PolyID" )  head(daily_weather)  clim_file <- file.path(clim_data_path, "climate-data-daily.csv") writeClimateData(daily_weather, clim_file) ## 1. Build an elevation raster covering the study area (lon-lat). elev <- get_elevation_rast(ecoregionPolys, z = 9)  ## 2. Tile cells of `elev` that fall inside `ecoregionPolys` into batches. ##    Each row carries Longitude/Latitude/Elevation, plus EcoID (from the ##    studyArea field named by `id`) and BatchID (for parallel dispatch). locations_batch <- create_locations_df(   elev = elev,   studyArea = ecoregionPolys,   id = "PolyID",   batch_size = 500 )  ## 3. Fetch one (batch, year) at a time. Cap parallelism to <= 4 workers ##    so we don't overload the BioSIM web service. The cache is namespaced ##    by a short hash of `studyArea` so distinct study areas don't collide. future::plan(future::multisession, workers = 2)  sa_hash <- landisutils:::.studyArea_hash(ecoregionPolys)  grid <- expand.grid(batch = seq_along(locations_batch), year = clim_years) furrr::future_map2(grid$batch, grid$year, function(b, y) {   get_clim_daily(locations_batch[b], year = y, studyArea_hash = sa_hash, path = clim_data_path) })  ## 4. Read the partitioned Arrow CSV dataset, summarize by ecoregion, ##    and pivot to LANDIS-II Climate Library wide format. daily_weather <- assemble_climate_library_file(   dataset_path = file.path(clim_data_path, "ClimaticEx_Daily", sa_hash),   vars = c("Prcp", "Tmin", "Tmax"),   id_col = "EcoID" )

previous_headings: Historical weather data > Daily Climate Data (BioSIM)
what: Daily climate inputs for Social Climate Fire
title: Preparing Climate Data for Use With LANDIS-II
text: CSV prep_daily_weather() / assemble_climate_library_file() produces consumed LANDIS-II Climate Library (Daily_RandomYears / Daily_AverageAllYears time series). Social Climate Fire extension reads daily fire-weather indirectly Climate Library computes FWI internally. assemble_climate_library_file_scf() thin wrapper emits table lowercase Variable spellings used Social-Climate-Fire reference input (LTB_ClimateInputs_91_10_v2.csv): precip, maxtemp, mintemp, windspeed, winddirection. Climate Library parser case-insensitive, either spelling works input — pick whichever scenario template expects.
code: ## populate the BioSIM cache with the 5 SCF variables prep_daily_weather(   vars = c("prcp", "tmax", "tmin", "ws", "wnddir"),   years = clim_years,   studyArea = ecoregionPolys,   id = "PolyID" )  sa_hash <- landisutils:::.studyArea_hash(ecoregionPolys) scenario_tag <- landisutils:::.biosim_scenario_tag("RCP45", "RCM4")  scf_inputs <- assemble_climate_library_file_scf(   dataset_path = file.path(clim_data_path, "ClimaticEx_Daily", sa_hash, scenario_tag),   vars = c("Prcp", "Tmin", "Tmax", "WndS", "WndD"),   id_col = "EcoID" )  clim_file <- file.path(clim_data_path, "scf-climate-inputs.csv") writeClimateData(scf_inputs, clim_file)

title: Preparing Climate Data for Use With LANDIS-II
text: , can get monthly data using following recipe: can also use recipe get monthly AET data prepare EcoregionParameters table:
code: climvars_monthly <- c("ppt", "tmax", "tmin")  monthly_weather <- prep_monthly_weather(   vars = climvars_monthly,   years = clim_years,   studyArea = ecoregionPolys,   id = "PolyID" )  head(monthly_weather)  clim_file <- file.path(clim_data_path, "climate-data-monthly.csv") writeClimateData(monthly_weather, clim_file) aet_df <- prep_monthly_weather(   vars = "aet",   years = clim_years,   studyArea = ecoregionPolys,   id = "PolyID" )  head(aet_df)  erp_df <- prepEcoregionParameters(aet_df)  head(erp_df)

title: Preparing Climate Data for Use With LANDIS-II
text: want monthly weather co-located daily BioSIM cells (elevation raster, point batches), use prep_monthly_weather_biosim() — shares elevation/batching machinery prep_daily_weather() fetches BioSIM’s Climatic_Monthly model. Wind variables (ws, wnddir) pulled call BioSIM’s companion ClimaticWind_Monthly model; wind -direction summarised across model’s 36 directional bins (W0..W350) using frequency-weighted circular mean: lower-level helpers (get_clim_monthly(), assemble_climate_library_file_monthly()) mirror daily pipeline need schedule per-batch fetches dynamic targets:
code: climvars_monthly_biosim <- c("prcp", "tmax", "tmin", "temp", "rh", "srad", "ws", "wnddir")  monthly_weather_biosim <- prep_monthly_weather_biosim(   vars = climvars_monthly_biosim,   years = clim_years,   studyArea = ecoregionPolys,   id = "PolyID" )  head(monthly_weather_biosim)  clim_file <- file.path(clim_data_path, "climate-data-monthly-biosim.csv") writeClimateData(monthly_weather_biosim, clim_file) elev <- get_elevation_rast(ecoregionPolys, z = 9) locations_batch <- create_locations_df(   elev = elev,   studyArea = ecoregionPolys,   id = "PolyID",   batch_size = 500 )  future::plan(future::multisession, workers = 2)  sa_hash <- landisutils:::.studyArea_hash(ecoregionPolys)  grid <- expand.grid(batch = seq_along(locations_batch), year = clim_years) furrr::future_map2(grid$batch, grid$year, function(b, y) {   get_clim_monthly(locations_batch[b], year = y, studyArea_hash = sa_hash, path = clim_data_path) })  monthly_weather_biosim <- assemble_climate_library_file_monthly(   dataset_path = file.path(clim_data_path, "Climatic_Monthly", sa_hash),   vars = c("TotalPrcp", "MeanTmin", "MeanTmax", "WndS", "WndD"),   id_col = "EcoID" )

title: Preparing Climate Data for Use With LANDIS-II
text: climr provides ClimateNA-style change-factor downscaling PRISM/DAYMET composite reference grid North America. Historical observational years span 1901-2024 (obs_ts_dataset = "climatena" "cru.gpcc"). lower-level helpers (get_clim_monthly_climr(), assemble_climate_library_file_monthly_climr()) mirror BioSIM pipeline need schedule per-year fetches dynamic targets. climr’s -disk cache configured via options("climr.cache.path" = ...); prep_monthly_weather_climr() defaults climr/ subdirectory getOption("landisutils.cache.path") duration call.
code: climvars_monthly_climr <- c("prcp", "tmax", "tmin", "temp", "rh", "cmd")  monthly_weather_climr <- prep_monthly_weather_climr(   vars = climvars_monthly_climr,   years = clim_years,   studyArea = ecoregionPolys,   id = "PolyID" )  head(monthly_weather_climr)  clim_file <- file.path(clim_data_path, "climate-data-monthly-climr.csv") writeClimateData(monthly_weather_climr, clim_file)

title: Preparing Climate Data for Use With LANDIS-II
text: prep_*() functions used historical/observational weather can also produce future weather climate-change scenarios. Two complementary sources wired : BioSIM (CMIP5-era): prep_daily_weather() prep_monthly_weather_biosim() accept rcp clim_model arguments forwarded BioSIM::generateWeather(). rcp one "CONSTANT_CLIMATE", "RCP45", "RCP85"; clim_model one "RCM4" (Canadian Regional Climate Model 4, CanESM2-driven), "GCM4" (CGCM4 / CanESM2-family GCM), "Hadley" (HadGEM). climr (CMIP6): prep_monthly_weather_climr() accepts gcms, ssps, max_run arguments forwarded climr::downscale(). Pass subset climr::list_gcms() (use bcgov-recommended 8-member subset exposed climr_ensemble_8) subset climr::list_ssps(). BioSIM climr caches namespaced scenario, historical future fetches study area coexist disk without colliding.

title: Preparing Climate Data for Use With LANDIS-II
text: Pass rcp clim_model straight prep_daily_weather(). Canadian fourth-generation GCM (CGCM4 / CanESM2-family) corresponds BioSIM’s clim_model = "GCM4": arguments also accepted prep_monthly_weather_biosim(), lets pull wind (ws, wnddir) shortwave-radiation (srad) variables matching scenario - climr expose either, pair BioSIM need wind radiation future projection.
code: climvars_daily <- c("prcp", "tmax", "tmin")  daily_weather_future <- prep_daily_weather(   vars = climvars_daily,   years = clim_years_future,   studyArea = ecoregionPolys,   id = "PolyID",   rcp = "RCP45",   clim_model = "GCM4" )  head(daily_weather_future)  clim_file <- file.path(clim_data_path, "climate-data-daily-rcp45-canesm.csv") writeClimateData(daily_weather_future, clim_file)

title: Preparing Climate Data for Use With LANDIS-II
text: prep_monthly_weather_climr() switches projection mode whenever gcms non-NULL. bcgov-recommended 8-member subset climr::list_gcms() exposed convenience climr_ensemble_8; see https://bcgov.github.io/climr/articles/guidance_ensembleSelection.html rationale. SSP2-4.5 ("ssp245") standard CMIP6 analogue CMIP5 RCP 4.5: row multi-GCM ensemble mean across 8 members, taken inside assemble_climate_library_file_monthly_climr(), output (Year, Month, Variable, <ecoregion-id>...) shape observational pipeline. Set max_run > 0 additionally average multiple runs per GCM (climr defaults ensemble-mean , max_run = 0).
code: climvars_monthly_climr <- c("prcp", "tmax", "tmin", "temp", "rh", "cmd")  monthly_weather_climr_future <- prep_monthly_weather_climr(   vars = climvars_monthly_climr,   years = clim_years_future,   studyArea = ecoregionPolys,   id = "PolyID",   gcms = climr_ensemble_8,   ssps = "ssp245",   max_run = 0L )  head(monthly_weather_climr_future)  clim_file <- file.path(clim_data_path, "climate-data-monthly-climr-ssp245-ens8.csv") writeClimateData(monthly_weather_climr_future, clim_file)

title: Biomass Browse Extension — BiomassBrowse
text: SpeciesTable data.frame six columns enumerated .browseSpeciesTableCols. ZoneMap Character. Relative path population-zones raster. BrowseMethod Character. One "Population", "BDI". DefinedPopulationFile Character. Relative path. DynamicPopulation List dynamic-population parameters, NULL. Required keys (numeric): RMin, RMax, MortalityMin, MortalityMax, PredationMin, PredationMax, HarvestMin, HarvestMax. ConsumptionRate Integer. ANPPForageProp Numeric [0, 1]. MinBrowsePropinReach Numeric [0, 1]. BrowseBiomassThreshold Numeric [0, 1]. BrowseBiomassThresholdMin Numeric [0, 1]. BrowseBiomassThresholdMax Numeric [0, 1]. EscapeBrowsePropLong Numeric [0, 1]. CalibrateMode Character ""/"". GrowthReduction Character ""/"". Mortality Character ""/"". CountNonForageinSitePref Character "TRUE"/"FALSE" (set logical). UseInitBiomassAsForage Character "TRUE"/"FALSE" (set logical). ForageInReachMethod One "Ordered", "LinearEachCohort". ForageQuantity Integer (neighborhood radius). SitePreference Integer (neighborhood radius). SitePrefMapNames Character. Output filename pattern. SiteForageMapNames Character. Output filename pattern. SiteHSIMapNames Character. Output filename pattern. SitePopulationMapNames Character. Output filename pattern. BiomassRemovedMapNames Character. Output filename pattern. LogFile Character. Relative file path. output_files Character vector output files (relative paths scenario directory) extension expected produce run time. Subclasses override return extension-specific log files, event CSVs, etc. paths collected scenario() written output_manifest.txt tar_landis() can track explicitly without relying list.files() discovery. Map files whose names depend timestep (e.g. BiomassC-10.tif) included ; discovered tar_landis()'s output_dir scan instead.

title: Biomass Browse Extension — BiomassBrowse
code: BiomassBrowse$new(   path,   Timestep = 1L,   SpeciesTable = NULL,   ZoneMap = NULL,   BrowseMethod = "Population",   DefinedPopulationFile = NULL,   DynamicPopulation = NULL,   ConsumptionRate = NULL,   ANPPForageProp = 0.66,   MinBrowsePropinReach = NULL,   BrowseBiomassThreshold = NULL,   BrowseBiomassThresholdMin = NULL,   BrowseBiomassThresholdMax = NULL,   EscapeBrowsePropLong = NULL,   CalibrateMode = NULL,   GrowthReduction = NULL,   Mortality = NULL,   CountNonForageinSitePref = NULL,   UseInitBiomassAsForage = NULL,   ForageInReachMethod = NULL,   ForageQuantity = NULL,   SitePreference = NULL,   SitePrefMapNames = NULL,   SiteForageMapNames = NULL,   SiteHSIMapNames = NULL,   SitePopulationMapNames = NULL,   BiomassRemovedMapNames = NULL,   LogFile = "browse/browse-log.csv" )

title: Biomass Browse Extension — BiomassBrowse
text: path Character. Directory path. Timestep Integer. Years browse updates (typically 1; §4.2.1). SpeciesTable data.frame six columns matching §4.2.2: Species (character), Preference, GrowthReductionThreshold, GrowthReductionMaximum, MortalityThreshold, MortalityMaximum (numeric [0, 1]). ZoneMap Character. Relative path population-zones raster (§4.2.3). BrowseMethod Character. One "Population" "BDI" (§4.2.4). DefinedPopulationFile Character. Relative path population / BDI definition file (§4.2.5). DynamicPopulation List dynamic-population parameters (§4.2.6) running dynamic mode; pass NULL static/defined population. Required keys (numeric): RMin, RMax, MortalityMin, MortalityMax, PredationMin, PredationMax, HarvestMin, HarvestMax. ConsumptionRate Integer (kg/yr/individual; §4.2.7). ANPPForageProp Numeric [0, 1]; proportion ANPP counts forage (§4.2.8; default 0.66). MinBrowsePropinReach Numeric [0, 1]; minimum proportion browse within reach cohort browsed (§4.2.9). BrowseBiomassThreshold Numeric [0, 1]; proportion ecoregion max biomass cohorts begin escape browse (§4.2.12). BrowseBiomassThresholdMin, BrowseBiomassThresholdMax (Optional) Numeric [0, 1]; thresholds ForageInReachMethod = "LinearEachCohort" form (§4.2.10–4.2.11). EscapeBrowsePropLong Numeric [0, 1]; proportion longevity cohorts considered escaped browse (§4.2.13). CalibrateMode Character ("" / ""); optional, default "" (§4.2.14). GrowthReduction Character ("" / ""); optional, default "" (§4.2.15). Mortality Character ("" / ""); optional, default "" (§4.2.16). CountNonForageinSitePref Logical; optional, default FALSE (§4.2.17). UseInitBiomassAsForage Logical; optional, default FALSE (§4.2.18). ForageInReachMethod Character; one "Ordered" "LinearEachCohort"; optional, default "Ordered" (§4.2.19). ForageQuantity (Optional) Integer; HSI neighborhood radius forage-quantity component (§4.2.20.1). Either SitePreference () must provided compute HSI maps. SitePreference (Optional) Integer; HSI neighborhood radius site-preference component (§4.2.20.2). SitePrefMapNames, SiteForageMapNames, SiteHSIMapNames, SitePopulationMapNames, BiomassRemovedMapNames (Optional) Character. Output filename patterns; must contain {timestep} (§4.2.21). LogFile Character. Relative path events CSV log (§4.2.22).

what: DGS (DAMM-McNiP, GIPL, SHAW) Succession Extension — DGSSuccession
title: DGS (DAMM-McNiP, GIPL, SHAW) Succession Extension — DGSSuccession
text: R6 class representing LANDIS-II DGS Succession (v2.0) extension's main input file. DGS integrates NECN-style vegetation dynamics model DAMM-McNiP soil C/N model, SHAW physically-based hydrology / energy balance model, GIPL deep-soil permafrost model. DGS extension must run Social Climate Fire (v4.01) Output Reclass extension (v4.0).

title: DGS (DAMM-McNiP, GIPL, SHAW) Succession Extension — DGSSuccession
text: LANDIS-II DGS Succession v2.0 Extension User Guide https://github.com/LANDIS-II-Foundation/Extension-DGS-Succession/blob/master/docs/LANDIS-II%20DGS%20Succession%20v2.0%20User%20Guide.pdf

title: DGS (DAMM-McNiP, GIPL, SHAW) Succession Extension — DGSSuccession

title: DGS (DAMM-McNiP, GIPL, SHAW) Succession Extension — DGSSuccession
text: CalibrateMode Logical, character indicating "yes" "". ClimateConfigFile Character. Relative file path. AtmosphericNSlope Numeric. AtmosphericNIntercept Numeric. InitialCommunities Character. Relative file path. InitialCommunitiesMap Character. Relative file path. Latitude Numeric (degrees). ShawGiplConfigFile Character. Relative file path. ShawGiplFiles Character vector relative file paths. SoilMaps Named list relative file paths. Required keys: SoilDepthMapName, SoilDrainMapName, SoilBaseFlowMapName, SoilStormFlowMapName, SoilFieldCapacityMapName, SoilWiltingPointMapName, SoilPercentSandMapName, SoilPercentClayMapName, SoilBulkDensityMapName, SoilParticleDensityMapName, InitialSOC_PrimaryMapName, InitialSON_PrimaryMapName, InitialDeadWoodSurfaceMapName, InitialDeadCoarseRootsMapName. InitialFineFuels Numeric [0, 1]. InitialMineralN Numeric (g m-2). DenitrificationRate Numeric [0, 1]. WaterDecayFunction Character. One "Linear" "Ratio". DammMcNIPParameters Named list numeric scalar parameters. Required keys: InitialMicrobialC, InitialMicrobialN, InitialEnzymeConc, ActEnergySOMDepoly, ActEnergyDOCUptake, ExpConstSOMDepoly, ExpConstDOCUptake, FractionSOMUnprotect, CNEnzymes, KmSOMDepoly, KmDOCUptake, EnzTurnRate, MicrobialTurnRate, CarbonUseEfficiency, PropEnzymeSOM, PropCEnzymeProduction, PropNEnzymeProduction, FractDeadMicrobialBiomassSOM, MMConstantO2, DiffConstantO2, DiffConstantSOMLiquid, FractionVolumeO2, DOCFraction, DONFraction, FractionLitterToDOC. SeedingAlgorithm Character. Dispersal algorithm use. One "WardSeedDispersal", "NoDispersal", "UniversalDispersal". ProbabilityEstablishAdjust Numeric. Default 1.0. SpeciesParameters Character. Relative file path. FireReductionParameters data.frame. HarvestReductionParameters data.frame.

code: DGSSuccession$new(   path,   Timestep = 1L,   CalibrateMode = NULL,   ClimateConfigFile = NULL,   AtmosphericNSlope = NULL,   AtmosphericNIntercept = NULL,   InitialCommunities = NULL,   InitialCommunitiesMap = NULL,   Latitude = NULL,   ShawGiplConfigFile = NULL,   ShawGiplFiles = NULL,   SoilMaps = NULL,   InitialFineFuels = NULL,   InitialMineralN = NULL,   DenitrificationRate = NULL,   WaterDecayFunction = NULL,   DammMcNIPParameters = NULL,   SeedingAlgorithm = NULL,   ProbabilityEstablishAdjust = 1,   SpeciesParameters = NULL,   FireReductionParameters = NULL,   HarvestReductionParameters = NULL )

title: DGS (DAMM-McNiP, GIPL, SHAW) Succession Extension — DGSSuccession
text: path Character. Directory path extension input files. Timestep Integer (years). CalibrateMode Logical, character indicating "yes" "". TRUE, additional DGS-calibrate-log.csv produced; intended single-cell runs . ClimateConfigFile Character. Relative file path climate library configuration file (see §2.4 user guide). AtmosphericNSlope Numeric. Slope linear N-deposition model (Total N = AtmosphericNSlope * precipitation + AtmosphericNIntercept). user-guide prose refers AtmosNslope, parser keyword AtmosphericNSlope. AtmosphericNIntercept Numeric. Intercept linear N-deposition model. user-guide prose calls AtmosNinter, parser keyword AtmosphericNIntercept. InitialCommunities Character. Relative file path initial communities text file (see §7 user guide). InitialCommunitiesMap Character. Relative file path initial communities raster. Latitude Numeric. Study-site latitude (degrees). ShawGiplConfigFile Character. Relative file path SHAW/GIPL configuration file names SHAW/GIPL input files (see §3 user guide). ShawGiplFiles (Optional) Character vector additional SHAW/GIPL input files (e.g., ListThus, ShawGeneralInputs, ShawPlantTypes, ShawSoilTypes, GiplProperties, Unfrozen.txt) register scenario can collect . SoilMaps Named list relative file paths required soil physical initial soil C/N / dead-wood map inputs. Required keys: SoilDepthMapName, SoilDrainMapName, SoilBaseFlowMapName, SoilStormFlowMapName, SoilFieldCapacityMapName, SoilWiltingPointMapName, SoilPercentSandMapName, SoilPercentClayMapName, SoilBulkDensityMapName, SoilParticleDensityMapName, InitialSOC_PrimaryMapName, InitialSON_PrimaryMapName, InitialDeadWoodSurfaceMapName, InitialDeadCoarseRootsMapName. InitialFineFuels Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction initial dead wood allocated fine fuels. InitialMineralN Numeric. Initial mineral N ($g/m^2$). DenitrificationRate Numeric [0, 1]. Monthly fraction mineral N lost volatilization denitrification. WaterDecayFunction Character. One "Linear" "Ratio". DammMcNIPParameters Named list numeric scalar parameters DAMM-McNiP soil C/N block. Required keys (must supplied): InitialMicrobialC, InitialMicrobialN, InitialEnzymeConc, ActEnergySOMDepoly, ActEnergyDOCUptake, ExpConstSOMDepoly, ExpConstDOCUptake, FractionSOMUnprotect, CNEnzymes, KmSOMDepoly, KmDOCUptake, EnzTurnRate, MicrobialTurnRate, CarbonUseEfficiency, PropEnzymeSOM, PropCEnzymeProduction, PropNEnzymeProduction, FractDeadMicrobialBiomassSOM, MMConstantO2, DiffConstantO2, DiffConstantSOMLiquid, FractionVolumeO2, DOCFraction, DONFraction, FractionLitterToDOC. SeedingAlgorithm Character. Dispersal algorithm use. One "WardSeedDispersal", "NoDispersal", "UniversalDispersal". ProbabilityEstablishAdjust Numeric. Multiplier applied probability establishment. Default 1.0. SpeciesParameters Character. Relative file path species-parameters CSV (see §2.54 user guide). FireReductionParameters data.frame columns FireSeverity, CoarseDebrisReduction, FineLitterReduction, CohortWoodReduction, CohortLeafReduction, OrganicHorizonReduction. Required even fire extension used. HarvestReductionParameters (Optional) data.frame columns PrescriptionName, DeadWoodReduction, DeadLitterReduction, SOMReduction, CohortWoodRemoval, CohortLeafRemoval. SOMReduction column required parser even though user guide (§2.56) list .

text: path Character. Directory path. Timestep Integer. Years. InputUnitsEnglish Logical ("yes"/""). TRUE, wind speeds mph; otherwise km/h. HurricaneRandomNumberSeed Optional integer. Seed hurricane-specific RNG; lets hurricanes vary independently Core RNG. Omit fall back Core seed. StormOccurrenceProbabilities data.frame columns Storms (integer) Probability (numeric); probabilities must sum 1.0. LowBoundLandfallWindSpeed, ModeLandfallWindSpeed, HighBoundLandfallWindSpeed Numeric. Landfall wind-speed distribution parameters (units per InputUnitsEnglish). CoastalSlope Numeric. Slope factor relative landscape. MeanStormIntersectionX, MeanStormIntersectionY Numeric. Mean storm-centre coordinates. LandfallSigma Numeric. Variance landfalls around mean. StormDirectionMu, StormDirectionSigma Numeric. Mean standard deviation storm direction (degrees). MinimumWindSpeedforDamage Numeric. Damage threshold; match smallest wind threshold WindSpeedVulnerabilities. ExposureMaps data.frame columns Degree (integer) MapName (character). WindSpeedVulnerabilities List WindSpeedVulnerability sub-objects (see windSpeedVulnerability()). MapNames Character. Output filename pattern; must contain literal {timestep}. Including {stormNumber} recommended per-storm maps within timestep overwrite one another. LogFile Character. Relative file path CSV log. WindReductionTableCSV Optional character. Relative path wind-reduction-factor CSV (links Output Cohort Statistics Evenness calculation). Omit disable structure-based wind-speed reduction.

InitialCommunitiesMap Character. Relative file path. ClimateConfigFile Character. Relative file path. SoilMaps Named list relative file paths. Required keys: SoilDepthMapName, SoilDrainMapName, SoilBaseFlowMapName, SoilStormFlowMapName, SoilFieldCapacityMapName, SoilWiltingPointMapName, SoilPercentSandMapName, SoilPercentClayMapName, InitialSOM1CsurfMapName, InitialSOM1NsurfMapName, InitialSOM1CsoilMapName, InitialSOM1NsoilMapName, InitialSOM2CMapName, InitialSOM2NMapName, InitialSOM3CMapName, InitialSOM3NMapName, InitialDeadWoodSurfaceMapName, InitialDeadCoarseRootsMapName. OptionalClimateMaps Named list relative file paths. Allowed keys: SlopeMapName, AspectMapName, NormalSWAMapName, NormalCWDMapName, NormalTempMapName. CalibrateMode Logical, character indicating "yes" "". SmokeModelOutputs Logical, character indicating "yes" "". Write_SWA_Maps Logical, character indicating "yes" "". Write_CWD_Maps Logical, character indicating "yes" "". Write_Temperature_Maps Logical, character indicating "yes" "". Write_Species_Drought_Maps Logical, character indicating "yes" "". WaterDecayFunction Character. One "Linear" "Ratio". ProbabilityEstablishAdjust Numeric. InitialMineralN Numeric (g m-2). InitialFineFuels Numeric [0, 1]. AtmosphericNSlope Numeric. AtmosphericNIntercept Numeric. Latitude Numeric (degrees). DenitrificationRate Numeric [0, 1]. DecayRateSurf Numeric [0, 1]. DecayRateSOM1 Numeric [0, 1]. DecayRateSOM2 Numeric [0, 1]. DecayRateSOM3 Numeric [0, 1]. GrassThresholdMultiplier Numeric. OutputMaps Named list. See table 3 user guide. Allowed names: ANPPMapName, ANPPMapFrequency, ANEEMapName, ANEEMapFrequency, SoilCarbonMapName, SoilNitrogenMapName, TotalCMapName. *MapName values file-name templates must contain literal {timestep} placeholder (user guide §2.29) – LANDIS-II replaces simulation year, e.g. "NECN/ANPP-{timestep}.tif". CreateInputCommunityMaps Logical, character indicating "yes" "". VariableOverrides Named list. Allowed names: Stormflow, WaterFactor1, WaterFactor2, AnaerobicFactor1, AnaerobicFactor2, AnaerobicFactor3. SpeciesParameters Character. Relative file path. DroughtMortalityParameters Character. Relative file path. FireReductionParameters data.frame. HarvestReductionParameters data.frame.

title: NECN Succession Extension — NECNSuccession
text: path Character. Directory path. Timestep Integer. SeedingAlgorithm Character. Dispersal algorithm use. One "WardSeedDispersal", "NoDispersal", "UniversalDispersal". InitialCommunitiesCSV Character. Relative file path initial communities CSV (see §4 user guide). InitialCommunitiesMap Character. Relative file path initial communities raster. ClimateConfigFile Character. Relative file path climate library configuration file. SoilMaps Named list relative file paths required soil physical initial soil organic matter / dead wood map inputs. Keys must match NECN keywords (Tables 1 2 user guide): SoilDepthMapName, SoilDrainMapName, SoilBaseFlowMapName, SoilStormFlowMapName, SoilFieldCapacityMapName, SoilWiltingPointMapName, SoilPercentSandMapName, SoilPercentClayMapName, InitialSOM1CsurfMapName, InitialSOM1NsurfMapName, InitialSOM1CsoilMapName, InitialSOM1NsoilMapName, InitialSOM2CMapName, InitialSOM2NMapName, InitialSOM3CMapName, InitialSOM3NMapName, InitialDeadWoodSurfaceMapName, InitialDeadCoarseRootsMapName. OptionalClimateMaps (Optional) Named list relative file paths SlopeMapName, AspectMapName, NormalSWAMapName, NormalCWDMapName, NormalTempMapName. CalibrateMode Logical, character indicating "yes" "". SmokeModelOutputs Logical, character indicating "yes" "". Write_SWA_Maps Logical, character indicating "yes" "". Write_CWD_Maps Logical, character indicating "yes" "". Write_Temperature_Maps Logical, character indicating "yes" "". Write_Species_Drought_Maps Logical, character indicating "yes" "". WaterDecayFunction Character. One "Linear" "Ratio". ProbabilityEstablishAdjust Numeric. Multiplier applied probability establishment. Default 1.0. InitialMineralN Numeric. Initial mineral N ($g/m^2$). InitialFineFuels Numeric [0, 1]. Fraction initial dead wood allocated fine fuels. AtmosphericNSlope Numeric. Slope linear N-deposition model. AtmosphericNIntercept Numeric. Intercept linear N-deposition model. Latitude Numeric. Study-site latitude (degrees). DenitrificationRate Numeric [0, 1]. Monthly fraction mineral N lost ammonia volatilization denitrification. DecayRateSurf Numeric [0, 1]. Max decay rate SOM1-surface pool. DecayRateSOM1 Numeric [0, 1]. Max decay rate SOM1-soil pool. DecayRateSOM2 Numeric [0, 1]. Max decay rate SOM2 (slow) pool. DecayRateSOM3 Numeric [0, 1]. Max decay rate SOM3 (passive) pool. GrassThresholdMultiplier (Optional) Numeric. Grass/tree competition multiplier; see user guide §2.28. OutputMaps (Optional) Named list optional output-map keywords values. See table 3 user guide. Allowed names: ANPPMapName, ANPPMapFrequency, ANEEMapName, ANEEMapFrequency, SoilCarbonMapName, SoilNitrogenMapName, TotalCMapName. *MapName values file-name templates must contain literal {timestep} placeholder (user guide §2.29), e.g. "NECN/ANPP-{timestep}.tif". CreateInputCommunityMaps Logical, character indicating "yes" "". VariableOverrides (Optional) Named list numeric override values. Allowed names: Stormflow, WaterFactor1, WaterFactor2, AnaerobicFactor1, AnaerobicFactor2, AnaerobicFactor3. SpeciesParameters Character. Relative file path species-parameters CSV (see §2.32, Table 4, user guide). DroughtMortalityParameters (Optional) Character. Relative file path drought-mortality-parameters CSV (see §2.33, Table 5, user guide). FireReductionParameters data.frame columns FireSeverity, CoarseDebrisReduction, FineLitterReduction, CohortWoodReduction, CohortLeafReduction, SOMReduction. Required even fire extension used. HarvestReductionParameters (Optional) data.frame columns PrescriptionName, DeadWoodReduction, DeadLitterReduction, CohortWoodRemoval, CohortLeafRemoval.

title: Landscape Habitat Output Extension — OutputLandscapeHabitat
text: LocalVariables (Optional) Named list data.frames. DerivedLocalVariables (Optional) Named character vector. NeighborhoodVariables (Optional) data.frame. ClimateVariables (Optional) data.frame. DistanceVariables (Optional) data.frame. SpeciesModels Named list data.frames columns Parameter, Type, Value. LocalVarMapFileNames Character. Must contain literal placeholders {local-var-name} {timestep}. NeighborVarMapFileNames Character. Must contain literal placeholders {neighbor-var-name} {timestep}. ClimateVarMapFileNames Character. Must contain literal placeholders {climate-var-name} {timestep}. DistanceVarMapFileNames Character. Must contain literal placeholders {distance-var-name} {timestep}. SpeciesMapFileNames Character. Must contain literal placeholders {species-name} {timestep}. SpeciesLogFileNames Character. Must contain literal placeholder {species-name}. LogFile Character. output_files Character vector output files (relative paths scenario directory) extension expected produce run time. Subclasses override return extension-specific log files, event CSVs, etc. paths collected scenario() written output_manifest.txt tar_landis() can track explicitly without relying list.files() discovery. Map files whose names depend timestep (e.g. BiomassC-10.tif) included ; discovered tar_landis()'s output_dir scan instead.

title: Landscape Habitat Output Extension — OutputLandscapeHabitat
text: path Character. Directory path. Timestep Integer. Years updates. LocalVariables (Optional) Named list mapping reclass map names data frames columns ForestType, AgeRange, Species. row defines one forest-type/age/species rule. DerivedLocalVariables (Optional) Named character vector. Names derived-variable names; values arithmetic expressions referencing local variables (e.g. "reclass2[LowlandCon] + reclass2[LowlandHdwd]"). NeighborhoodVariables (Optional) data.frame columns Name, LocalVar, NeighborRadius, Transform. ClimateVariables (Optional) data.frame columns Name, Year, Months, Source, ClimateVar, Transform. DistanceVariables (Optional) data.frame columns Name, LocalVar, Transform. SpeciesModels Named list. name species name; value data.frame columns Parameter, Type, Value describing logistic-regression terms (use "intercept" intercept row). LocalVarMapFileNames (Optional) Character. Output template local-variable maps; must contain literal placeholders {local-var-name} {timestep}. NeighborVarMapFileNames (Optional) Character. Output template neighborhood maps; must contain literal placeholders {neighbor-var-name} {timestep}. ClimateVarMapFileNames (Optional) Character. Output template climate maps; must contain literal placeholders {climate-var-name} {timestep}. DistanceVarMapFileNames (Optional) Character. Output template distance maps; must contain literal placeholders {distance-var-name} {timestep}. SpeciesMapFileNames (Optional) Character. Output template species-model maps; must contain literal placeholders {species-name} {timestep}. SpeciesLogFileNames (Optional) Character. Output template species log files; must contain literal placeholder {species-name}. LogFile Character. Relative file path main log.

title: PnET-Succession Extension — PnETSuccession
text: path Character. Directory path. Timestep Integer. Succession timestep years (recommended <= 10). StartYear Integer. Climate year simulation begins. SeedingAlgorithm Character. Dispersal algorithm use. One "WardSeedDispersal", "NoDispersal", "UniversalDispersal". Latitude (Optional) Numeric. Global study-site latitude degrees. Can alternatively set per-ecoregion EcoregionParameters file. PNEToutputsites (Optional) Character. Relative file path PNEToutputsites input file (see §12 user guide). InitialCommunities Character. Relative file path initial communities CSV (see §4 user guide). InitialCommunitiesMap Character. Relative file path initial communities raster. LitterMap (Optional) Character. Relative file path initial leaf litter map ($g/m^2$). WoodyDebrisMap (Optional) Character. Relative file path initial dead woody debris map ($g/m^2$). PnETGenericParameters (Optional) Character. Relative file path PnET generic parameters file (§7). PnETSpeciesParameters Character. Relative file path PnET species parameters file (§8). EcoregionParameters Character. Relative file path PnET ecoregion parameters file (§9). DisturbanceReductions (Optional) Character. Relative file path disturbance reductions file (§10). ClimateConfigFile (Optional) Character. Relative file path LANDIS-II climate library configuration file. omitted, climate file(s) referenced EcoregionParameters used. SaxtonAndRawlsParameters (Optional) Character. Relative file path override Saxton--Rawls soil parameter file. CohortBinSize (Optional) Integer. Number years represented age cohort; must >= Timestep.

LethalTemp Numeric <= 0 (°C). Minimum air temperature pathogen survive. MinSoilTemp Numeric (°C). Minimum soil temperature pathogen transition Uninfected -> Infected. PhWet Numeric. Pressure head (wet condition threshold). PhDry Numeric. Pressure head (dry condition threshold). PhMax Numeric. Pressure head (maximum threshold). MinProbID Numeric [0, 1]. MaxProbDI Numeric [0, 1]. Relative file path events CSV log; NULL disables. SummaryLog (Optional) Character. Relative file path summary CSV log; NULL disables. output_files Character vector output files (relative paths scenario directory) extension expected produce run time.
