title: Root Rot Extension — RootRot
text: path Character. Directory path. Timestep Integer. Years updates. Pathogen (Optional) Character. Pathogen name annotate alongside LandisData header (e.g. "Phytophthora cinnamomi"). InputMap (Optional) Character. Initial infection raster path. omitted, model assumes cells begin Uninfected. SpeciesSusceptibility data.frame columns Species, Index1 (initial susceptibility), Index2 (secondary susceptibility); indices [0, 1]. LethalTemp Numeric <= 0 (°C). Minimum air temperature pathogen survive. MinSoilTemp Numeric (°C). Minimum soil temperature pathogen transition Uninfected → Infected. PhWet, PhDry, PhMax Numeric > 0 (m). Pressure-head thresholds: wet, dry, extremely-dry conditions. MinProbID, MaxProbDI Numeric [0, 1]. Min infection→diseased probability max diseased→infected probability. OutputMapName (Optional) Character. Infection-status output raster pattern; must contain {timestep}. TOLDMapName (Optional) Character. Time--Last-Disease output raster pattern; must contain {timestep}. LethalTempMapName (Optional) Character. Lethal-temperature output raster pattern; must contain {timestep}. TotalBiomassRemovedMapName (Optional) Character. Total biomass- removed output raster pattern; must contain {timestep}. SpeciesBiomassRemovedMapName (Optional) Character. Per-species biomass-removed output raster pattern; must contain {species} {timestep}. EventLog (Optional) Character. Relative file path events CSV log; pass NULL disable. SummaryLog (Optional) Character. Relative file path summary CSV log; pass NULL disable.

(2019). landscape model variable social-ecological fire regimes. Ecological Modelling, 401, 85–93. doi:10.1016/j.ecolmodel.2019.03.022

text: LandisExtension -> SocialClimateFire

title: Social-Climate-Fire Extension — SocialClimateFire
text: TimeZeroPET (Optional) Real. Potential Evapotranspiration (PET) time zero. TimeZeroCWD (Optional) Real. Climate Water Deficit (CWD) time zero. Species_CSV_File Character. Relative file path. AccidentalIgnitionsMap Character. Relative file path. DynamicAccidentalIgnitionMaps (Optional) data.frame columns Year FileName. LightningIgnitionsMap Character. Relative file path. DynamicLightningIgnitionMaps (Optional) data.frame columns Year FileName. RxIgnitionsMap Character. Relative file path. DynamicRxIgnitionMaps (Optional) data.frame columns Year FileName. AccidentalSuppressionMap Character. Relative file path. LightningSuppressionMap Character. Relative file path. RxSuppressionMap Character. Relative file path. DynamicAccidentalSuppressionMaps (Optional) data.frame columns Year FileName. GroundSlopeMap Character. Relative file path. UphillSlopeAzimuthMap Character. Relative file path. ClayMap Character. Relative file path. LightningIgnitionsCoeffs Real. Parameters B0 B1 equation 1 (Scheller et al. 2019). AccidentalIgnitionsCoeffs Real. B0 B1 equation 1 (Scheller et al. 2019). IgnitionDistribution Character. One "Poisson" "ZeroInflatedPoisson". LightningIgnitionsBinomialCoeffs Real. Parameters $b_z_0$ $b_z_1$ equation 2 (Scheller et al. 2019). AccidentalIgnitionsBinomialCoeffs Real. Parameters $b_z_0$ $b_z_1$ equation 2 (Scheller et al. 2019). MaximumFineFuels Real. Maximum amount fine fuels ($g/m^2$). MaximumRxWindSpeed Real. Maximum wind speed ($m/s$) prescribed fires. MaximumRxFireWeatherIndex (Optional) Real. Maximum Fire Weather Index (FWI) prescribed fires. MinimumRxFireWeatherIndex (Optional) Real. Minimum Fire Weather Index (FWI) prescribed fires. MaximumRxTemperature (Optional) Real. Maximum temperature prescribed fires. MinimumRxRelativeHumidity (Optional) Real. Minimum relative humidity prescribed fires. MaximumRxFireIntensity Integer. Maximum allowable fire intensity prescribed fires. NumberRxAnnualFires Integer. Number prescribed fires attempted per year. NumberRxDailyFires Integer. Number prescribed fires attempted per day. FirstDayRxFires Integer. First Julian day prescribed fire can begin. LastDayRxFires Integer. Last Julian day prescribed fire can begin. TargetRxSize Real. Maximum size prescribed fire ($ha$). RxZonesMap (Optional) Character. Relative file path. MaximumSpreadAreaCoeffs Real. Parameters B0, B1, B2 equation 4 (Scheller et al. 2019). SpreadProbabilityCoeffs Real. Parameters B0, B1, B2 equation 3 (Scheller et al. 2019). SiteMortalityCoeffs Real. Parameters B0, B1, B2, B3, B4, B5, B6 equation 7 (Scheller et al. 2019). CohortMortalityCoeffs Real. Parameters B0, B1, B2 equation 10 (Scheller et al. 2019). LadderFuelMaxAge Integer. Maximum age ladder fuels. LadderFuelSpeciesList Character. Vector species codes considered ladder fuels. SuppressionMaxWindSpeed Real. Maximum wind speed ($m/s$) fire suppression. Suppression_CSV_File Character. Relative file path. DeadWoodTable data.frame columns Species Age.

PRISM (https://prism.oregonstate.edu/) applies topographically-aware interpolation (slope, aspect, elevation, coastal proximity, temperature inversions) downscaling station observations grid (Daly et al. 2008); sources wrapped use simpler interpolation schemes can produce large discrepancies areas complex topography (mountainous terrain, steep elevation gradients, rain shadows): inter-product differences 5-60% annual precipitation (Henn et al. 2018) >6 \(^\circ\)C temperature (Walton & Hall 2018) documented western US. Carefully evaluate suitability chosen source study area, especially topographically heterogeneous landscapes.

code: bdaAgent(   name,   BDPCalibrator = NULL,   SRDMode = NULL,   StartYear = NULL,   EndYear = NULL,   OutbreakPattern = NULL,   Mean = NULL,   StDev = NULL,   MaxInterval = NULL,   MinInterval = NULL,   TimeSinceLastEpidemic = NULL,   TemporalType = NULL,   MinROS = NULL,   MaxROS = NULL,   Dispersal = NULL,   DispersalRate = NULL,   EpidemicThresh = NULL,   InitialEpicenterNum = NULL,   OutbreakEpicenterCoeff = NULL,   OutbreakEpicenterThresh = NULL,   SeedEpicenter = NULL,   SeedEpicenterMax = NULL,   SeedEpicenterCoeff = NULL,   DispersalTemplate = NULL,   NeighborFlag = NULL,   NeighborSpeedUp = NULL,   NeighborRadius = NULL,   NeighborShape = NULL,   NeighborWeight = NULL,   IntensityClass2_BDP = NULL,   IntensityClass3_BDP = NULL,   EcoregionModifiers = NULL,   DisturbanceModifiers = NULL,   BDASpeciesParameters = NULL,   IgnoredSpecies = NULL )

title: Construct a Climate BDA agent — bdaAgent
text: name Character. Agent name (spaces); used BDAAgentName basename per-agent input file. BDPCalibrator Numeric. BDP calibrator. SRDMode Character. One "max" "mean". StartYear, EndYear (Optional) Integer. Outbreak window. OutbreakPattern Character. One "CyclicNormal" "CyclicUniform". Mean, StDev Numeric. Required OutbreakPattern = "CyclicNormal". MaxInterval, MinInterval Numeric. Required OutbreakPattern = "CyclicUniform". TimeSinceLastEpidemic Integer. Years. TemporalType Character. One "pulse" "variablepulse". MinROS, MaxROS Integer. Region--spread bounds. Dispersal Logical / "yes" / "". Whether dispersal enabled. DispersalRate Numeric. Meters/year. Required Dispersal truthy. EpidemicThresh Numeric. InitialEpicenterNum Integer. OutbreakEpicenterCoeff, OutbreakEpicenterThresh Numeric. SeedEpicenter Logical / "yes" / "". SeedEpicenterMax (Optional, v5+) Integer. SeedEpicenterCoeff Numeric. Required SeedEpicenter truthy. DispersalTemplate Character. One "MaxRadius", "4N", "8N", "12N", "24N". NeighborFlag Logical / "yes" / "". Whether neighborhood resource calculation enabled. NeighborSpeedUp Character. One "none", "2x", "3x", "4x". Required NeighborFlag truthy. NeighborRadius Numeric. Meters. Required NeighborFlag truthy. NeighborShape Character. One "uniform", "linear", "gaussian". Required NeighborFlag truthy. NeighborWeight Numeric. Required NeighborFlag truthy. IntensityClass2_BDP, IntensityClass3_BDP Numeric. BDP thresholds intensity classes 2 3 (class 1 hardwired 0.0). EcoregionModifiers (Optional) data.frame columns Ecoregion, CapacityModifier. DisturbanceModifiers (Optional) data.frame columns SRDModifier, Duration, Type. Type column may contain space-separated disturbance type names. BDASpeciesParameters data.frame columns Species, MinorHostAge, MinorHostSRDProb, SecondHostAge, SecondHostSRDProb, MajorHostAge, MajorHostSRDProb, Class3Age, Class3VulnProb, Class2Age, Class2VulnProb, Class1Age, Class1VulnProb, CFSConifer. IgnoredSpecies (Optional) Character vector species codes exclude BDA disturbance.

title: Construct a Climate BDA agent — bdaAgent
text: list class "BDAAgent".

title: Build the calibration scenario template directory — build_calibration_scenario_template
text: out_dir Character. Destination directory (created overwritten). template_dir Character. Existing production fire scenario directory copy . snapshot_ic_csv, snapshot_ic_tif Character. Paths spun-community CSV / TIF (return build_calibration_spinup_scenario()). baseline_fire_size_table, baseline_fuel_type_table, baseline_fire_damage_table, baseline_seasons_sim_table data.frame NULL. Baseline (uncalibrated) tables. four supplied, function writes fresh dynamic-fire.txt . sim_years Integer. Calibration sim duration (years). Default 10. cell_length Integer. Keys output filenames (relative out_dir); values paths source files copy place whatever copied template_dir. Accepted keys: "ground_slope.tif", "uphill_slope_azimuth.tif", "fire-ecoregions.tif", "initial_weather_database.csv", "DynamicFire_Spp_Table.csv", "species.txt", "ecoregions.txt", "ecoregions.tif", "climate.txt". .tif overrides also copy .aux.xml / .tfw sidecars present alongside source.

title: Build the calibration scenario template directory — build_calibration_scenario_template
text: Composition: template's succession backend (ForC Succession Biomass Succession, auto-detected), frozen calibration: ForCS gets frozen Timestep + DOM-spinup-/biomass-spinup-flags; Biomass Succession just gets frozen Timestep (SpinUp section). Either way succession effectively -op fire behaviour reflects candidate parameters, vegetation change. Dynamic Fire System + Dynamic Fuel System disturbances. Initial communities point spun-snapshot build_calibration_spinup_scenario() (renamed standard initial-communities.csv + .tif existing ForCS config references work without modification). Duration = sim_years. baseline fire-config tables supplied (recommended), function overwrites copied dynamic-fire.txt fresh uncalibrated config built tables. breaks otherwise-circular dependency production fire config calibration loop (production fire config -> calibrated_fire_params -> calibration -> production fire config).

title: Build a calibration spinup scenario directory — build_calibration_spinup_scenario
text: out_dir Character. Destination directory (created overwritten). template_dir Character. Existing production scenario directory copy . Must contain forc-succession.txt, species.txt, ecoregions.txt, ecoregions.tif, climate.txt, ForCS_DM.txt, initial-communities.csv, initial-communities.tif, ForCS data CSVs. duration Integer. Simulation duration years. LANDIS-II minimum 1. community_output_year Integer. Year snapshot consumed downstream (caller chooses 0 post-spinup state; default 0). cell_length Integer. Raster cell size metres.

title: Build a calibration spinup scenario directory — build_calibration_spinup_scenario
text: Character scalar: absolute path written scenario.txt.

title: Build a calibration spinup scenario directory — build_calibration_spinup_scenario
text: Output Biomass Community extension emits multiples Timestep starting year 0 (post-init, pre-step), Timestep = 1 Duration = 1 get two snapshot CSVs: community-input-file-0.csv (post-spinup state – one want) community-input-file-1.csv (one year ANPP). TIF (output-community-0.tif) emitted year 0 – cohort communities repartition -disturbance run, one raster suffices years. Note CSV schema: LANDIS-II Output Biomass Community v3 writes 5-column file (MapCode, SpeciesName, CohortAge, CohortBiomass, CohortANPP). LANDIS-II's initial-communities parser tolerates extra CohortANPP column, file drop-usable InitialCommunitiesFiles without post-processing. Implementation: copy every top-level file template_dir (production scenario directory), strip disturbance stack copied scenario.txt, add Output Biomass Community extension, rewrite scenario.txt Duration = duration. Rep subdirectories copied.

what: DEoptim driver for Dynamic Fire calibration — calibrate_dynamic_fire
title: DEoptim driver for Dynamic Fire calibration — calibrate_dynamic_fire
text: Sets warm Docker pool (landis simulator Docker) FORK cluster n_cores workers, invokes DEoptim::DEoptim() multi-component loss objective. Pool + cluster torn via .exit() regardless success / error / interrupt.

title: DEoptim driver for Dynamic Fire calibration — calibrate_dynamic_fire
DEoptim driver for Dynamic Fire calibration — calibrate_dynamic_fire
text: observed_targets_path Character. Path .rds save_observed_fire_targets(). scenario_template Character. Path calibration scenario's scenario.txt (return build_calibration_scenario_template()). cfg List. Calibration config. Expected keys: lower, upper Named numeric vectors keyed calibration_par_names(). NP, itermax, strategy DEoptim control args. reltol, steptol Optional DEoptim early-stopping controls. set, DEoptim halts itermax best--population objective fails improve reltol steptol consecutive generations. Defaults: reltol = 1e-3 (0.1% relative improvement) steptol = 25 generations. Pass steptol = itermax (value >= itermax) disable early stopping always run full schedule. Pass cfg$steptol = NULL fall back upstream DEoptim default (steptol = itermax). n_reps, sim_years, weights, base_seed Per-trial settings. n_cores, parallel Parallelism settings. simulator "landis" (default), "r_reimpl", "mock". method "docker" (default) "local". image, cpu_limit, mem_limit, pull Pool settings (Docker ). out_dir Character. write DEoptim trace + scratch sub-directory. Created missing.

title: DEoptim driver for Dynamic Fire calibration — calibrate_dynamic_fire
text: List best_params (named numeric), objective (scalar), deoptim (full DEoptim return), trace_path (per-iter best-value CSV path), trial_trace_path (per-trial loss-decomposition CSV path, one row per objfn evaluation; columns: wall_clock_iso, pid, par_<name>..., total, comp_<name>..., w_<name>..., weighted_<name>.... Useful plotting DEoptim trades four loss components iterations.), cfg (echo), pool_image / pool_digest (provenance; NA pool started).

title: DEoptim driver for Dynamic Fire calibration — calibrate_dynamic_fire

what: Robust BioSIM::generateWeather() wrapper: request staggering + exponential-backoff retry — .biosim_generate_weather
title: Robust BioSIM::generateWeather() wrapper: request staggering + exponential-backoff retry — .biosim_generate_weather
text: BioSIM web API can slow transiently unavailable many requests arrive , BioSIM R client exposes timeout/retry/delay knobs (biosimclient.config() tunes nbNearestNeighbours climate-generation/test flags). bare generateWeather() call therefore protection: transient failure aborts whole fetch, stalled call hangs indefinitely. wrapper adds (tunable via options()): random pre-request stagger delay concurrent furrr workers hit server lockstep – landisutils.biosim.request_delay = c(min, max) seconds (default c(1, 5)); exponential backoff jitter failure – landisutils.biosim.max_attempts (default 5L) total tries, waiting landisutils.biosim.backoff_base * 2^(attempt - 1) seconds (base default 15) tries, repeated failures back progressively (gentler overwhelmed server); optional per-attempt timeout – landisutils.biosim.timeout seconds (default Inf = ) via base::setTimeLimit(); timeout J4R client reset (BioSIM::shutdownClient()) next try stalled socket poison .

title: Robust BioSIM::generateWeather() wrapper: request staggering + exponential-backoff retry — .biosim_generate_weather

what: Patch a ForC Succession config for calibration use (internal) — .patch_forcs_for_calibration
title: Patch a ForC Succession config for calibration use (internal) — .patch_forcs_for_calibration
text: Two surgical replacements forc-succession.txt: Timestep N -> Timestep <sim_years + 1>. Makes ForCS skip succession step duration calibration run (growth, establishment, mortality) DEoptim trial purely fire--fixed-landscape experiment. SpinUp data row -> 0  0  1  20. calibration IC already post-spinup (snapshot build_calibration_spinup_scenario()), per-trial spinup wasted compute. DOM equilibration also skipped – fire severity depends cohort biomass, DOM.

code: edaAgent(   name,   SHIMode = "mean",   StartYear = NULL,   EndYear = NULL,   TransmissionRate = NULL,   AcquisitionRate = NULL,   InitialEpidemMap = NULL,   DispersalType = "STATIC",   DispersalKernel = "PowerLaw",   DispersalMaxDist = NULL,   AlphaCoef = NULL,   EDASpeciesParameters = NULL,   DisturbanceModifiers = NULL,   IgnoredSpecies = NULL,   extraClimateLines = character(0) )

title: Construct an EDA agent — edaAgent
text: name Character. Agent name (spaces). SHIMode Character. One "mean" "max". StartYear, EndYear (Optional) Integer. Outbreak window. TransmissionRate Numeric (beta0). Mean rate infected cell infects another cell per timestep. AcquisitionRate Numeric (rD). Rate acquisition detectable symptoms per timestep. InitialEpidemMap (Optional) Character. Relative path initial-outbreak raster. DispersalType Character. One "STATIC" "DYNAMIC". DispersalKernel Character. One "PowerLaw" "NegExp". DispersalMaxDist Numeric. Cut-distance (m) dispersal kernel. AlphaCoef Numeric. Exponent dispersal kernel. EDASpeciesParameters data.frame required columns: Species, LowAge, LowScore, MediumAge, MediumScore, HighAge, HighScore, VulnLowAge, VulnLowMortProb, VulnMediumAge, VulnMediumMortProb, VulnHighAge, VulnHighMortProb, CFSConifer, MortalityPlot. DisturbanceModifiers (Optional) data.frame columns SHIModifier, Duration, Type. IgnoredSpecies (Optional) Character vector species codes exclude SHI calculation. extraClimateLines (Optional) Character vector raw lines climate-input section (e.g. ClimateVariables table). Inserted verbatim, transmission block.

title: Construct an EDA agent — edaAgent

what: Fetch a single year of daily BioSIM weather for one location batch — get_clim_daily
title: Fetch a single year of daily BioSIM weather for one location batch — get_clim_daily
text: Wraps BioSIM::generateWeather() ClimaticEx_Daily model, fetching one calendar year cells locations_batch[[1]]. Output appended Arrow CSV dataset path/ClimaticEx_Daily/<studyArea_hash>/<rcp>_<clim_model>/, partitioned YEAR/BatchID. function intended run dynamic target across many (batch, year) combinations; existing partition files detected via file.exists() skipped, re-runs cheap.

title: Fetch a single year of daily BioSIM weather for one location batch — get_clim_daily
code: get_clim_daily(   locations_batch,   year,   studyArea_hash,   path = .climateCachePath(),   rcp = "RCP45",   clim_model = "RCM4" )

title: Fetch a single year of daily BioSIM weather for one location batch — get_clim_daily
text: locations_batch list whose first element data frame columns ID, Latitude, Longitude, Elevation, EcoID, BatchID. Typically one element create_locations_df()'s output, wrapped list. year integer single calendar year fetch. studyArea_hash character. Short hash study-area object (see .studyArea_hash()) used cache subdirectory distinct study areas collide. path character. Directory ClimaticEx_Daily/ arrow dataset written. Default uses package climate cache (getOption("landisutils.cache.path")). rcp character. BioSIM representative concentration pathway. One "CONSTANT_CLIMATE", "RCP45", "RCP85". Default "RCP45" (BioSIM's default). clim_model character. BioSIM climate model. One "RCM4", "GCM4", "Hadley". Default "RCM4" (BioSIM's default).

title: Fetch a single year of daily BioSIM weather for one location batch — get_clim_daily
text: Path (character) partition CSV (year, BatchID).

title: Fetch a single year of daily BioSIM weather for one location batch — get_clim_daily
text: years within BioSIM's observational range, rcp clim_model effect returned data, still namespace cache. future years select CMIP5-era projection: rcp one "CONSTANT_CLIMATE", "RCP45", "RCP85"; clim_model one "RCM4" (Canadian Regional Climate Model 4, CanESM2-driven), "GCM4" (CGCM4 / CanESM2-family GCM), "Hadley" (HadGEM). Rate-limiting BioSIM web service caller's responsibility - cap parallel workers (e.g. future::plan(multisession, workers = 4)) stagger orchestrator calls. function performs internal throttling.

title: Fetch a single year of daily BioSIM weather for one location batch — get_clim_daily
text: climate data sources used package (BioSIM, Daymet, TerraClim) PRISM-derived. PRISM (https://prism.oregonstate.edu/) applies topographically-aware interpolation (slope, aspect, elevation, coastal proximity, temperature inversions) downscaling station observations grid (Daly et al. 2008); sources wrapped use simpler interpolation schemes can produce large discrepancies areas complex topography (mountainous terrain, steep elevation gradients, rain shadows): inter-product differences 5-60% annual precipitation (Henn et al. 2018) >6 \(^\circ\)C temperature (Walton & Hall 2018) documented western US. Carefully evaluate suitability chosen source study area, especially topographically heterogeneous landscapes.

title: Fetch a single year of daily BioSIM weather for one location batch — get_clim_daily
text: Daly, C., Halbleib, M., Smith, J.., Gibson, W.P., Doggett, M.K., Taylor, G.H., Curtis, J., & Pasteris, P.P. (2008). Physiographically sensitive mapping climatological temperature precipitation across conterminous United States. International Journal Climatology, 28(15), 2031-2064. doi:10.1002/joc.1688 Henn, B., Newman, .J., Livneh, B., Daly, C., & Lundquist, J.D. (2018). assessment differences gridded precipitation datasets complex terrain. Journal Hydrology, 556, 1205-1219. doi:10.1016/j.jhydrol.2017.03.008

what: Fetch one year of monthly climr weather and write Arrow CSV partitions — get_clim_monthly_climr
title: Fetch one year of monthly climr weather and write Arrow CSV partitions — get_clim_monthly_climr
text: Wraps climr::downscale() one calendar year points xyz, splits long-format output base variable writes Arrow CSV partitions path/Climr_Monthly/<studyArea_hash>/<scenario_tag>/. Partitions keyed Variable/Year observational mode additionally GCM/SSP projection mode. Existing per-variable / per-year partition trees detected via dir.exists() skipped, re-runs cheap.

title: Fetch one year of monthly climr weather and write Arrow CSV partitions — get_clim_monthly_climr
code: get_clim_monthly_climr(   year,   xyz,   vars,   studyArea_hash,   obs_ts_dataset = "climatena",   path = .climateCachePath(),   gcms = NULL,   ssps = NULL,   max_run = 0L )

title: Fetch one year of monthly climr weather and write Arrow CSV partitions — get_clim_monthly_climr
text: year integer single calendar year fetch. projection mode, must lie within climr::list_gcm_ssp_years(). xyz data.frame columns lon, lat, elev, id (one row per cell). See .climr_build_xyz() (internal) build manually. vars character vector lowercase variable names (e.g. c("prcp", "tmax", "tmin")); see prep_monthly_weather_climr() supported set. studyArea_hash character. Short hash study-area object (see .studyArea_hash()) used cache subdirectory distinct study areas collide. obs_ts_dataset character. climr observational time-series dataset. One "climatena" "cru.gpcc". Default "climatena". Ignored gcms set. path character. Directory Climr_Monthly/ arrow dataset written. Default uses package climate cache (getOption("landisutils.cache.path")). gcms character vector CMIP6 GCM names (subset climr::list_gcms()). NULL (default), runs observational mode. set, runs projection mode ssps required. ssps character vector CMIP6 SSP names (subset climr::list_ssps(), e.g. "ssp245"). Required gcms set. max_run integer number ensemble runs per GCM fetch. ensemble_mean = TRUE (climr's default use), max_run = 0L returns per-GCM ensemble mean. Default 0L.

title: Fetch one year of monthly climr weather and write Arrow CSV partitions — get_clim_monthly_climr
text: Path (character) scenario-tagged dataset directory (.e. directory containing Variable=... partitions).

title: Fetch one year of monthly climr weather and write Arrow CSV partitions — get_clim_monthly_climr
text: function runs two modes: Observational (default) gcms = NULL. Uses obs_years = year obs_ts_dataset ClimateNA-style historical anomalies. Projection gcms set (e.g. climr_ensemble_8) ssps set (e.g. "ssp245"). Uses gcm_ssp_years = year fetch CMIP6 GCM time-series projections; max_run controls many runs per GCM averaged (ensemble_mean = TRUE, climr's default). Per climr API, climr cache configured setting options("climr.cache.path" = ...) (climr GitHub issue 274). orchestrator prep_monthly_weather_climr() sets sensible default via withr::local_options() duration call; call helper directly .

Carefully evaluate suitability chosen source study area, especially topographically heterogeneous landscapes.

Get an elevation raster for a study area — get_elevation_rast
text: studyArea sf SpatVector polygons object delineating area interest. z integer zoom level passed elevatr::get_elev_raster(); higher values give finer resolution. Defaults <landisutils.cache.path>/elevatr_tiles/, keeps AWS Terrain Tile fragments inside package cache instead leaking R session's global tempdir() (elevatr's default).

title: Fetch a single year of daily BioSIM Fire Weather Index for one location batch — get_fwi_daily
text: BioSIM's FWI_Daily model known occasionally produce implausibly large FFMC, ISI, FWI, BUI values (see https://github.com/RNCan/BioSimClient_R/issues/14). work around , FFMC values \(>\) 101 recomputed underlying T/RH/WS/Prcp columns using cffdrs, BUI recomputed DMC/DC, ISI FWI recomputed corrected FFMC BUI. unused DSR column dropped. Rate-limiting BioSIM web service caller's responsibility - cap parallel workers (e.g. future::plan(multisession, workers = 4)) stagger orchestrator calls. small random delay (5-30 s) applied per call spread BioSIM hits many parallel workers start .

Carefully evaluate suitability chosen source study area, especially topographically heterogeneous landscapes.

text: Returns validated prescription specification. One passed BiomassHarvest via Prescriptions.

title: Construct a Biomass Harvest prescription — harvestPrescription
code: harvestPrescription(   name,   StandRanking = NULL,   EconomicRankTable = NULL,   FireHazardTable = NULL,   TimeSinceLastFire = NULL,   TimeSinceLastWind = NULL,   MinimumAge = NULL,   MaximumAge = NULL,   StandAdjacency = NULL,   AdjacencyType = NULL,   AdjacencyNeighborSetAside = NULL,   MinimumTimeSinceLastHarvest = NULL,   ForestTypeTable = NULL,   PresalvageYears = NULL,   SiteSelection = NULL,   MinTargetSize = NULL,   MaxTargetSize = NULL,   PatchPercentage = NULL,   PatchSize = NULL,   AllowOverlap = NULL,   RepeatExactCells = NULL,   MinTimeSinceDamage = NULL,   PreventEstablishment = FALSE,   CohortsRemoved = NULL,   SpeciesList = NULL,   Plant = NULL,   SingleRepeat = NULL,   SingleRepeatCohortsRemoved = NULL,   SingleRepeatSpeciesList = NULL,   SingleRepeatPlant = NULL,   MultipleRepeat = NULL,   TimesToRepeat = NULL )

title: Construct a Biomass Harvest prescription — harvestPrescription
text: name Character. Prescription name (spaces); referenced HarvestImplementations. StandRanking Character. One "Economic", "MaxCohortAge", "RegulateAges", "Random", "FireHazard", "TimeSinceDisturbance". EconomicRankTable data.frame columns Species, EconomicRank, MinimumAge. Required StandRanking = "Economic". FireHazardTable data.frame columns FuelType, FuelTypeRank. Required StandRanking = "FireHazard". TimeSinceLastFire, TimeSinceLastWind Integer. Years. Exactly one required StandRanking = "TimeSinceDisturbance". MinimumAge, MaximumAge (Optional) Integer years. StandAdjacency (Optional) Integer. Years. AdjacencyType (Optional) Character. One "StandAge" "TimeSinceLastHarvested". AdjacencyNeighborSetAside (Optional) Integer. Years. MinimumTimeSinceLastHarvest (Optional) Integer. Years. ForestTypeTable (Optional) data.frame columns InclusionRule, AgeRange, PercentCells, Species. Multiple species may space-separated Species column. PresalvageYears (Optional) Integer. Years. SiteSelection Character. One "Complete", "CompleteStandSpread", "PartialStandSpread", "PatchCutting". MinTargetSize, MaxTargetSize Numeric (hectares). Required SiteSelection "CompleteStandSpread" "PartialStandSpread". PatchPercentage Numeric (0, 100]. Required SiteSelection = "PatchCutting". PatchSize Numeric (hectares). Required SiteSelection = "PatchCutting". AllowOverlap, RepeatExactCells (Optional) Logical. applies SiteSelection = "PatchCutting". MinTimeSinceDamage (Optional) Integer. PreventEstablishment Logical. TRUE, emits bare PreventEstablishment keyword. CohortsRemoved Character. One "ClearCut", "PlantOnly", "SpeciesList". SpeciesList data.frame columns Species Cohorts. Required CohortsRemoved = "SpeciesList". Plant (Optional) Character vector species codes plant harvest. SingleRepeat (Optional) Integer. Interval (years) single-repeat harvest. SingleRepeatCohortsRemoved (Optional) Character. CohortsRemoved method use repeat entry; required SingleRepeat set. SingleRepeatSpeciesList (Optional) data.frame like SpeciesList, used SingleRepeatCohortsRemoved = "SpeciesList". SingleRepeatPlant (Optional) Character vector species plant repeat entry. MultipleRepeat (Optional) Integer. Interval (years) multiple-repeat harvest. TimesToRepeat (Optional) Integer. Caps number multiple-repeat iterations.

title: Construct a Biomass Harvest prescription — harvestPrescription
text: list class "HarvestPrescription".

what: Host CPU and RAM identification (cross-platform) — host_cpu_info
title: Host CPU and RAM identification (cross-platform) — host_cpu_info
text: Identifies CPU model, logical core count, total RAM machine running current R process. field falls back NA determined.

title: Host CPU and RAM identification (cross-platform) — host_cpu_info
Host CPU and RAM identification (cross-platform) — host_cpu_info
text: list elements model (character), n_logical (integer), ram_bytes (numeric).

title: Host CPU and RAM identification (cross-platform) — host_cpu_info
text: Implementation platform: Linux: reads /proc/cpuinfo (model name) /proc/meminfo (MemTotal). macOS (Darwin): sysctl -n machdep.cpu.brand_string CPU model, sysctl -n hw.memsize RAM. Windows: PROCESSOR_IDENTIFIER environment variable CPU model, wmic ComputerSystem get TotalPhysicalMemory RAM. Logical core count uses parallel::detectCores() every platform. Used landis_run_docker() landis_run_local() append host context rep's resource log downstream provenance tooling can identify host produced given replicate's outputs.

Crucially COPY – scratch source stays complete, authoritative replicate destination verified, total transfer failure loses nothing. verified rsync exit status 0, staging directory published atomic rename final_dir. rename atomic, final_dir ever appears complete – partial transfer never visible downstream skip-check reads final_dir. (--remove-source-files deliberately used: delete scratch incrementally leave half-emptied source total failure, partial destination mistaken complete run.) final_dir place scratch source deleted. run_dir final_dir already resolve path (scratch use), -op returns final_dir without copying deleting. Used tar_landis() replicate separate targets branch; branches run concurrently sets directory, avoiding O(N_REPS) re-setup cost --. files supplied used explicit copy list; GDAL sidecar files (.tfw, .aux.xml) accompany .tif list included automatically. files NULL fallback every top-level file scenario_dir (sub-directories never copied). function idempotent: existing replicate directories left untouched, adding replicates later never alters previously-created ones. base_seed provided RandomNumberSeed line rep's scenario.txt rewritten base_seed + (rep_index - 1), giving every replicate distinct deterministic seed. seeds derived rep index (order creation), adding replicates later never changes existing seeds.

what: Run a LANDIS-II simulation inside a Docker container — landis_run_docker
title: Run a LANDIS-II simulation inside a Docker container — landis_run_docker
text: Runs LANDIS-II ephemeral Docker container, blocking simulation completes. scenario directory bind-mounted /sim inside container. Stdout stderr written <scenario_dir>/log/docker_stdout.log docker_stderr.log. Wall-clock elapsed time peak memory use (polled every 2 s docker stats) reported completion written <scenario_dir>/log/docker_resources.log. image's immutable sha256 digest captured <scenario_dir>/log/docker_image.log downstream provenance tools can pin runs specific image regardless subsequent tag movement.

title: Run a LANDIS-II simulation inside a Docker container — landis_run_docker
code: landis_run_docker(   scenario_dir,   scenario_file = "scenario.txt",   image = NULL,   console = NULL,   pull = FALSE,   cpu_limit = 2,   mem_limit = "8g",   mem_margin = 1.5,   post_completion_timeout_sec = 300,   startup_jitter = NULL )

title: Run a LANDIS-II simulation inside a Docker container — landis_run_docker
text: scenario_dir Character. Path scenario directory (resolved absolute mounting). scenario_file Character. Scenario filename relative scenario_dir. image Character NULL. Docker image reference. Defaults getOption("landisutils.docker.image") (set .onLoad() "ghcr.io/landis-ii-foundation/landis-ii-v8-release:main"). console Character NULL. Path Landis.Console.dll inside container. Defaults NULL, calls landis_find_docker() run time (reads getOption("landisutils.docker.console")). pull Logical. TRUE, run docker pull <image> simulation recorded digest reflects current registry rather stale local copy. Defaults FALSE keep runs reproducible across iterations already-cached image. cpu_limit Numeric NULL. Hard CPU cap container (docker run --cpus). Default 2: LANDIS-II compute effectively single-threaded – empirical measurement across 90 concurrent ForCS + Dynamic Fire + Dynamic Fuels containers shows median 1.00 cores used per container, p99 = 1.11 cores, max = 1.11 cores. .NET runtime hosts 9-11 OS threads simulator thread compute-bound; GC / threadpool helpers occasionally peek 1.0 cores brief bursts. 2 covers 99th-percentile burst ~80% headroom; 1 tight enough .NET GC helper contend simulator thread cycles. Pass NULL CPU limit. mem_limit Numeric byte count, character (e.g. "8g", "512m"), NULL, Inf. Baseline RAM cap (docker run --memory). Default "8g". prior <rep_dir>/log/*_resources.log exists recorded peak_mem_bytes, cap raised peak_mem_bytes * mem_margin exceeds baseline – rep fit last time never killed cap rerun. prior log exists rep (first run, rep dir freshly deleted), cap dropped entirely first run can discover needs. mem_margin Numeric. Headroom factor applied previously observed peak auto-raising mem_limit. Default 1.5. post_completion_timeout_sec Numeric. Grace period (seconds) string "Model run complete." first appears container's stdout watchdog SIGTERMs container. long ForCS + Dynamic Fire scenarios many output extensions log completion marker spin .NET runtime shutdown path indefinitely (outputs already disk, sim completed cleanly). timeout container stopped exit codes 137/143 remapped 0. Set Inf disable watchdog. Default 300 (5 min). startup_jitter Numeric seconds NULL. Upper bound random start delay applied function touches Docker, stagger container launches avoid thundering-herd surge Docker daemon (disk backing image layers / renv library) many replicates start crew. call sleeps runif(1, 0, startup_jitter) seconds. delay affect simulation results (identical seed inputs), run tar_landis() deliberately baked {targets} command – changing never invalidates completed replicates. NULL (default) reads LANDIS_STARTUP_JITTER environment variable (can set project .Rprofile crew workers inherit); unset/invalid value means 0 (stagger).

title: Run a LANDIS-II simulation inside a Docker container — landis_run_docker
text: Named list exit_code (integer), elapsed_sec (numeric), peak_mem_bytes (numeric), returned invisibly.

title: Run a LANDIS-II simulation inside a Docker container — landis_run_docker
text: Single container per replicate. container name derived deterministically (real) scenario directory, docker's name uniqueness acts cross-worker mutex: replicate dispatched two workers (example targets re-runs branch false-positive worker crash original container still running), second call start parallel container instead adopts running one: waits container finish (applying post-completion watchdog) returns result. duplicate dispatch thus becomes harmless wait rather two dotnet processes truncating 's outputs.

code: loss_from_stats(   reps,   observed,   weights = c(count = 1, size = 1, area_fuel = 0, severity = 0) )

title: Compute the calibration loss from N replicate trial outputs — loss_from_stats
text: reps List. element return value parse_dynamic_fire_logs() one replicate. observed List. Output save_observed_fire_targets(). Must contain $primary ($fru59 back-compat alias) $lambda_obs, $n_fires_by_year, $fire_sizes_ha. May contain $primary$area_by_fuel_ha, $primary$severity_dist, $fuel_code_to_base, $pixel_area_ha activate Tier 2 components. weights Named numeric vector. Components: count, size, area_fuel, severity. Missing components default 0.

title: Compute the calibration loss from N replicate trial outputs — loss_from_stats
text: Named list total (scalar minimised DEoptim), components (per-component contributions), weights (echoed weights).

title: Compute the calibration loss from N replicate trial outputs — loss_from_stats
text: Components: L_count = |mean(n_fires_sim) - lambda_obs| / sd(n_fires_obs) – annual-rate match primary ecoregion target. L_size = KS_D(empirical CDF sim sizes, empirical CDF obs sizes) – shape match fire-size distribution. L_area_fuel: chi-squared distance simulated observed burn-area--base-fuel-type proportions. Simulated area--fuel comes event's ignition fuel code times DamagedSites, mapped base fuel types via observed$fuel_code_to_base. Skipped (contributes 0) either observed$primary$area_by_fuel_ha NULL observed$fuel_code_to_base missing. L_severity: chi-squared distance simulated observed severity-class proportions. Simulated severities come event's MeanSeverity binned integer classes 1..5; observed comes observed$primary$severity_dist (5-element named numeric vector summing 1). Skipped observed NULL. component values unitless non-negative; chi-squared components use small epsilon denominator avoid division zero empty observed bins.

Shared across ecoregion rows – per-ecoregion HiProp calibration require 6 params 3. FuelTypeTable data rows: column 4 (IgnProb) multiplied base-type-specific candidate (e.g., IgnProb_Conifer Base == "Conifer"). Default IgnProbs mostly 1.0 (D1 = 0.5), candidate range [0, 1.5] directly scales relative-weighting.

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
text: Download prepare climate data use LANDIS-II simulations.

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
code: prep_daily_weather(   vars = NULL,   years = NULL,   studyArea = NULL,   id = NULL,   batch_size = 1000,   z = 9,   rcp = "RCP45",   clim_model = "RCM4" )  prep_monthly_weather_biosim(   vars = NULL,   years = NULL,   studyArea = NULL,   id = NULL,   batch_size = 1000,   z = 9,   ref_grid = NULL,   rcp = "RCP45",   clim_model = "RCM4" )  prep_monthly_weather_climr(   vars = NULL,   years = NULL,   studyArea = NULL,   id = NULL,   batch_size = 1000,   z = 9,   obs_ts_dataset = c("climatena", "cru.gpcc"),   gcms = NULL,   ssps = NULL,   max_run = 0L )  prep_monthly_weather(vars = NULL, years = NULL, studyArea = NULL, id = NULL)

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
text: vars character specifying climate variables (lowercase, e.g. "prcp", "tmax", "tmin"). years integer vector specifying years. studyArea sf polygons object delineating e.g., ecoregions fire zones. id character specifying name column/field studyArea identifying ecoregions. batch_size integer maximum cells per BioSIM batch call. Default 1000. Smaller values yield parallel-friendly chunks. z integer zoom level passed elevatr::get_elev_raster() underlying elevation raster. Higher values give finer raster (point cells fed BioSIM/climr); lower values give coarser raster (fewer cells, fewer batches, faster fetches). Default 9 (~250 m mid-latitudes). tests / smoke runs, values around 5-7 typically yield single BioSIM batch small study area. rcp character. BioSIM representative concentration pathway. One "CONSTANT_CLIMATE", "RCP45", "RCP85". Default "RCP45". used BioSIM pipelines (prep_daily_weather(), prep_monthly_weather_biosim()). See Climate projections (BioSIM, CMIP5). clim_model character. BioSIM climate model. One "RCM4", "GCM4", "Hadley". Default "RCM4". used BioSIM pipelines. ref_grid optional fixed reference SpatRaster GLOBAL per-cell cache. cell numbers taken STABLE GLOBAL ids cell VALUES read BioSIM elevation (elevM) – MUST carry elevation values (e.g. DEM resampled onto grid). supplied, monthly pull cached global ids one shared, accumulating store, overlapping / nested study areas reuse cells already fetched. NULL (default), per-study-area elevatr grid (z) used. obs_ts_dataset character. climr observational time-series dataset. One "climatena" "cru.gpcc". Default "climatena". used prep_monthly_weather_climr() observational mode. gcms character vector CMIP6 GCM names (subset climr::list_gcms()). NULL (default), prep_monthly_weather_climr() runs observational mode; set, projection mode. bcgov-recommended eight-member ensemble exposed climr_ensemble_8. used prep_monthly_weather_climr(). ssps character vector CMIP6 SSP names (subset climr::list_ssps(), e.g. "ssp245"). Required gcms set. used prep_monthly_weather_climr(). max_run integer number ensemble runs per GCM fetch climr (averaged together ensemble_mean = TRUE, climr default). Default 0L (climr's default; uses ensemble mean ). used prep_monthly_weather_climr().

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
text: tbl_df columns Year, Month, Variable, one column per ecoregion (named id values). Daily output (prep_daily_weather()) additionally carries Day column.

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
text: prep_daily_weather() retrieves daily weather BioSIM (ClimaticEx_Daily model) cells studyArea, summarizes ecoregion (id), pivots LANDIS-II Climate Library wide format. Internally chains get_elevation_rast(), create_locations_df(), get_clim_daily() (per batch / per year), assemble_climate_library_file(). Per-batch / per-year results cached Arrow CSV dataset getOption("landisutils.cache.path"), partitioned <rcp>_<clim_model>/YEAR/BatchID. prep_monthly_weather() retrieves monthly weather TerraClim via climateR package summarizes zone using zonal package. prep_monthly_weather_biosim() retrieves monthly weather BioSIM (Climatic_Monthly model) using point-batch / Arrow caching pipeline prep_daily_weather(), cells co-located daily workflow. Internally chains get_elevation_rast(), create_locations_df(), get_clim_monthly() (per batch / per year), assemble_climate_library_file_monthly(). Per-batch / per-year results cached Arrow CSV dataset getOption("landisutils.cache.path"), partitioned <rcp>_<clim_model>/YEAR/BatchID. prep_monthly_weather_climr() retrieves monthly weather climr, ClimateNA-style change-factor downscaler PRISM/DAYMET composite reference grid. Cells come elevation/point machinery BioSIM pipeline (get_elevation_rast(), create_locations_df()); single climr::downscale() call per year dispatched via furrr::future_map(). climr maintains -disk cache, configured via options("climr.cache.path" = ...); wrapper defaults climr/ subdirectory getOption("landisutils.cache.path") option unset.

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
text: BioSIM provides modelled daily North American weather. ClimaticEx_Daily model exposes columns Prcp, Tmin, Tmax, Tair, RelH, WndS, WndD, SRad. Pass corresponding lowercase variable names vars (e.g. c("prcp", "tmax", "tmin", "rh", "ws", "wnddir", "srad")).

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
text: Terra Climate provides monthly North American weather 1980-present (https://www.climatologylab.org/terraclimate.html). TerraClim variables: aet, def, PDSI, pet, ppt, q, soil, srad, swe, tmax, tmin, vap, vpd, ws. Use prep_monthly_weather() TerraClim weather data.

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
text: BioSIM's Climatic_Monthly model exposes columns TotalPrcp, MeanTmin, MeanTmax, MeanTair, MeanRelH, TotalRadiation (among others); wind variables come companion ClimaticWind_Monthly model fetched call (WindSpeed plus 36 directional-frequency columns W0..W350, frequency-weighted circular-mean wind -direction computed). Pass corresponding lowercase variable names vars (e.g. c("prcp", "tmax", "tmin", "temp", "rh", "srad", "ws", "wnddir")). Use prep_monthly_weather_biosim() BioSIM monthly weather.

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
text: climr provides ClimateNA-style change-factor downscaling PRISM/DAYMET composite reference grid North America, observational time series spanning 1901-2024 (obs_ts_dataset = "climatena" "cru.gpcc"). Supported lowercase vars: prcp, tmax, tmin, temp, rh, srad, cmd (climatic moisture deficit). Wind variables (ws, wnddir) provided climr - use prep_monthly_weather_biosim() wind. Use prep_monthly_weather_climr() climr monthly weather.

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
text: prep_daily_weather() prep_monthly_weather_biosim() accept rcp clim_model arguments forwarded BioSIM. rcp one "CONSTANT_CLIMATE" (1991-2020 climate replayed), "RCP45", "RCP85". clim_model one "RCM4" (Canadian Regional Climate Model 4, CanESM2-driven), "GCM4" (CGCM4 / CanESM2-family GCM), "Hadley" (HadGEM). years within BioSIM's observational range arguments effect returned data; always namespace -disk cache (<rcp>_<clim_model> subdirectory <studyArea_hash>/).

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
text: prep_monthly_weather_climr() accepts gcms, ssps, max_run arguments forwarded climr::downscale() CMIP6 GCM projections. gcms NULL function runs observational mode; otherwise runs projection mode using gcm_ssp_years = years. bcgov-recommended eight-member ensemble subset exposed package constant climr_ensemble_8; pass gcms = climr_ensemble_8 use . assemble_climate_library_file_monthly_climr() averages GCMs SSPs give single LANDIS-II-compatible value per (Year, Month, EcoID, Variable). See climr::list_gcms(), climr::list_ssps() full choice sets.

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
text: BioSIM remote web service. parallelizing batches/years (e.g. future::plan(multisession)), keep worker count modest (\(\le\) 4) avoid hammering service.

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
text: Per-batch / per-year BioSIM fetches cached Arrow CSV partitions directory given getOption("landisutils.cache.path"). Re-runs find existing partition skip BioSIM call. prep_monthly_weather_climr() relies climr's -disk cache instead; cache root getOption("climr.cache.path"), defaulting (duration call) file.path(getOption("landisutils.cache.path"), "climr") unset.

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
text: climate data sources used package (BioSIM, Daymet, TerraClim) PRISM-derived. PRISM (https://prism.oregonstate.edu/) applies topographically-aware interpolation (slope, aspect, elevation, coastal proximity, temperature inversions) downscaling station observations grid (Daly et al. 2008); sources wrapped use simpler interpolation schemes can produce large discrepancies areas complex topography (mountainous terrain, steep elevation gradients, rain shadows): inter-product differences 5-60% annual precipitation (Henn et al. 2018) >6 \(^\circ\)C temperature (Walton & Hall 2018) documented western US. Carefully evaluate suitability chosen source study area, especially topographically heterogeneous landscapes.

title: Prepare Climate Data — prep_daily_weather
text: Daly, C., Halbleib, M., Smith, J.., Gibson, W.P., Doggett, M.K., Taylor, G.H., Curtis, J., & Pasteris, P.P. (2008). Physiographically sensitive mapping climatological temperature precipitation across conterminous United States. International Journal Climatology, 28(15), 2031-2064. doi:10.1002/joc.1688 Henn, B., Newman, .J., Livneh, B., Daly, C., & Lundquist, J.D. (2018). assessment differences gridded precipitation datasets complex terrain. Journal Hydrology, 556, 1205-1219. doi:10.1016/j.jhydrol.2017.03.008

code: save_observed_fire_targets(   primary_points,   primary_polys,   fire_years,   fuel_types_rast,   path,   secondary_points = NULL,   secondary_polys = NULL,   primary_label = "primary",   secondary_label = "secondary",   fuel_code_to_base = bc_fuel_code_to_base(),   severity_dist = NULL )

title: Save observed fire-regime targets (NFDB-derived) for calibration loss — save_observed_fire_targets
text: primary_points SpatVector. NFDB ignition points primary ecoregion (LANDIS simulation extent). Required. primary_polys SpatVector NULL. Fire perimeter polygons primary ecoregion. supplied, fire_sizes_ha drawn polys' SIZE_HA (e.g. NBAC's ADJ_HA) area_by_fuel_ha computed rasterising polys fuel_types_rast. NULL, fire_sizes_ha falls back points' SIZE_HA (NFDB agency-reported sizes) area_by_fuel_ha NULL primary summary. fire_years Integer vector. Years counts normalised (denominator lambda_obs). fuel_types_rast SpatRaster. Integer-coded fuel-type raster covering LANDIS simulation extent. path Character. Output .rds path. Parent dir created missing. secondary_points, secondary_polys SpatVector NULL. , optional regional-context ecoregion. area_by_fuel_ha computed secondary (see Details). primary_label, secondary_label Character. Labels two ecoregions (e.g., "FRU59" / "FRT12"). Stored payload reproducibility. fuel_code_to_base Named character vector. Mapping fuel_types_rast integer codes (character names) five base fuel types defaultFuelTypeTable(). NA values mark non-fuel codes excluded. Default: bc_fuel_code_to_base(). severity_dist Named numeric vector NULL. Expected proportions across 5 Dynamic Fire severity classes (names "1".."5"). Stored primary-ecoregion summary; consumed loss_from_stats()'s L_severity component. NULL = skip severity calibration (loss contributes 0). literature-prior default, see default_severity_prior_sturtevant2009().

title: Save observed fire-regime targets (NFDB-derived) for calibration loss — save_observed_fire_targets
text: Character. Absolute path written file.

title: Save observed fire-regime targets (NFDB-derived) for calibration loss — save_observed_fire_targets
text: Loss-component consumers: L_count uses n_fires_by_year (mean + sd normalisation). L_size uses fire_sizes_ha (sorted vector; KS test sim). L_area_fuel uses primary-ecoregion area_by_fuel_ha (Tier 2; weight 0 Tier 1). L_severity stays NULL; populate literature priors Tier 2 severity matching implemented. Per ecoregion (primary_ecoregion, secondary_ecoregion): Fire counts come NFDB IGNITION POINTS (one row = one ignition). NFDB polygons sparser (mapped larger fires). Fire sizes come NFDB points' SIZE_HA column (zeros dropped keep lognormal-flavoured size distribution positive). area_by_fuel_ha computed PRIMARY ecoregion via polygon overlay fuel_types_rast. fuel_types_rast covers LANDIS simulation domain; secondary-ecoregion polygons typically extend well beyond extent, making secondary computation misleading (just primary value ).

code: tar_landis(   name,   scenario_dir,   rep_index,   deps = NULL,   scenario_file = "scenario.txt",   output_dir = "output",   method = NULL,   image = NULL,   console = NULL,   base_seed = NULL,   pull = FALSE,   force = FALSE,   cpu_limit = 2,   mem_limit = "8g",   mem_margin = 1.5,   post_completion_timeout_sec = 300,   work_root = NULL,   pattern = NULL,   packages = targets::tar_option_get("packages"),   library = targets::tar_option_get("library"),   error = targets::tar_option_get("error"),   memory = targets::tar_option_get("memory"),   resources = targets::tar_option_get("resources"),   storage = targets::tar_option_get("storage"),   retrieval = targets::tar_option_get("retrieval"),   cue = targets::tar_option_get("cue"),   description = targets::tar_option_get("description") )

title: Create a targets target that runs one LANDIS-II replicate — tar_landis
text: name Symbol (unquoted). Target name. scenario_dir Symbol expression (unquoted). Upstream target provides scenario directory path(s) run time. rep_index Symbol (unquoted). Upstream target provides 1-based integer index current branch. Must defined iteration = "vector" cross() iterates individual elements. Typically created tar_target(name = ..._rep_index, command = seq_len(n_reps), iteration = "vector"). deps List (unquoted, optional). list() upstream target symbols must complete simulation runs, e.g. list(landis_scenario_file, landis_ext_forcs_file). Values used directly – embedded command {targets} detects upstream dependencies. scenario_file Character. Scenario filename inside scenario_dir. output_dir Character vector. Output subdirectory (subdirectories) inside scenario_dir; files found (recursively) returned tracked outputs. Defaults "output". Pass c("output", "fire") using Dynamic Fire extension, writes maps logs fire/ subdirectory. method Character NULL. "docker" run Docker, "local" run via local dotnet installation. NULL (default) reads getOption("landisutils.run.method"), defaults "local" Windows "docker" Linux/macOS. image Character NULL. Docker image use (method = "docker" ). Resolved getOption("landisutils.docker.image") NULL. console Character NULL. Path Landis.Console.dll. method = "docker" path inside container; method = "local" local filesystem path (defaults landis_find()). base_seed Integer NULL. Passed landis_replicate(): non-NULL, RandomNumberSeed replicate's scenario.txt set base_seed + (rep_index - 1), giving run distinct deterministic seed. Adding replicates later never changes existing seeds seeds derived rep index, order creation. pull Logical. Passed landis_run_docker() method = "docker": TRUE, image docker pulled running digest captured log/docker_image.log reflects current registry. Defaults FALSE. effect method = "local". force Logical (default FALSE). FALSE, tar_landis() skips actual landis_run_*() call rep dir already contains completed Landis-log.txt log/input_hash.json sidecar whose recorded hash matches current inputs (per-input-file MD5 + base_seed rep_index + scenario_file). TRUE, skip check bypassed LANDIS-II invoked unconditionally. cpu_limit, mem_limit, mem_margin Passed landis_run_docker() method = "docker". See function's documentation semantics; defaults 2, "8g", 1.5 respectively. effect method = "local". post_completion_timeout_sec Numeric. Passed landis_run_docker() method = "docker": grace period (seconds) LANDIS-II console logs "Model run complete." container SIGTERMed dotnet exited . Guards known v8 post-completion hang (long ForCS + Dynamic Fire sims spin 100% CPU .NET shutdown path outputs already disk). Default 300 (5 min); pass Inf disable watchdog. effect method = "local". work_root Character NULL. Optional fast, local, Docker-bind- mountable scratch root used RUN replicate, separate final (tracked) scenario_dir. Needed scenario_dir lives storage Docker daemon bind-mount (e.g. root-squashed NFS share): rep staged + run work_root/<scenario>/<studyArea>/repNN, completed outputs moved scenario_dir/repNN via landis_archive_rep() (fault-tolerant rsync + retry) value target returns – everything {targets} tracks – FINAL location, scratch holding transient run files. skip-check output collection read final scenario_dir/repNN. NULL (default), per-run env var LANDIS_SCRATCH consulted run time ({crew} workers can set via .Rprofile); also empty rep runs place scenario_dir (original behaviour). effect method = "local". pattern Expression (unquoted). Dynamic-branching pattern covering scenario replicate dimensions, e.g. cross(landis_run_name, landis_run_output_rep_index). Passed directly targets::tar_target_raw(). packages, library, error, memory, resources, storage, retrieval, cue, description Standard {targets} options; default targets::tar_option_get().

title: Create a targets target that runs one LANDIS-II replicate — tar_landis
text: single tar_target object (targets::tar_target_raw()).

title: Create a targets target that runs one LANDIS-II replicate — tar_landis
text: Per-replicate parallel branching: caller creates rep-index target iteration = "vector" combines scenario dimension via cross(). Keeping rep-index target explicit project's list() makes visible static analysis tools (tarborist) makes iteration = "vector" annotation clear project code:   Caching: (scenario, replicate) branch cached independently. Adding replicates (increasing seq_len() value) computes new branches; existing ones remain untouched. method resolved factory-call time (.e., _targets.R evaluated) baked target command. ensures {crew} worker processes, inherit R session options, receive correct values. Set options(landisutils.run.method = ...) _local.R pipeline list constructed control method per machine.
code: list(   ## iteration = "vector" is critical: cross() iterates over each ELEMENT,   ## giving n_scenarios x n_reps independent branches dispatched in parallel.   tar_target(     name      = landis_run_output_rep_index,     command   = seq_len(5L),     iteration = "vector"   ),   landisutils::tar_landis(     name      = landis_run_output,     rep_index = landis_run_output_rep_index,     ...     pattern   = cross(landis_run_name, landis_run_output_rep_index)   ) )

what: Write a LANDIS-II scenario.txt file (lower-level helper) — write_landis_scenario_file
title: Write a LANDIS-II scenario.txt file (lower-level helper) — write_landis_scenario_file
text: Generates scenario.txt path using insert*() helpers. Lower-level scenario(): takes already-written extension config-file paths (named character vectors keyed extension name) rather R6 LandisExtension objects. Useful project pipelines write extension configs separate targets-managed steps just want assemble top-level scenario file paths.

title: Write a LANDIS-II scenario.txt file (lower-level helper) — write_landis_scenario_file
code: write_landis_scenario_file(   path,   duration,   cell_length,   species_file,   ecoregions_files,   succession_ext_files,   disturbance_ext_files = NULL,   other_ext_files = NULL,   output_manifest = character(0) )

title: Write a LANDIS-II scenario.txt file (lower-level helper) — write_landis_scenario_file
text: path Character. Scenario directory path (absolute relative). duration Integer. Simulation duration years. cell_length Integer. Raster cell size metres. species_file Character. Path core species input file. ecoregions_files Character vector length 2: paths ecoregions text file ecoregions raster map. succession_ext_files Named character vector. Names extension names (e.g. "ForC Succession"); values absolute paths per-extension init files. Exactly one entry required. disturbance_ext_files, other_ext_files Named character vector NULL. structure succession_ext_files; zero entries. output_manifest Character vector fixed-name output files (paths relative replicate directory) scenario produce run time. Written output_manifest.txt tar_landis() can track files alongside recursive output_dir scan.

what: Convert logical to a LANDIS-II yes/no or true/false token — yesno
title: Convert logical to a LANDIS-II yes/no or true/false token — yesno
text: LANDIS-II input files mostly use strings "yes" "" boolean parameters, extensions (e.g. Magic Harvest's NoHarvestReInitialization) require "true" / "false" instead.

title: Convert logical to a LANDIS-II yes/no or true/false token — yesno
Convert logical to a LANDIS-II yes/no or true/false token — yesno
text: x one TRUE, FALSE, "Y", "N", "yes", "", "T", "F", "true", "false" (case-insensitive).
