text: yesno(): character "yes" "". truefalse(): character "true" "false".

title: landisutils 0.0.47
text: calibrate_dynamic_fire() now resolves LANDIS-II input files pre-flight check requires scenario template contain – succession config, species file, Dynamic Fire inputs – template (new internal .calibration_required_files(), reusing .calibration_succession_backend() / .calibration_species_file() / .calibration_directive_file()) instead asserting one project’s fixed filenames. Templates build_calibration_scenario_template() writes Biomass Succession scenario (e.g. biomass-succession.txt, species-core.txt, initial-weather-database.csv, dynamic-fire-species.csv) previously failed check “missing required files: forc-succession.txt, species.txt, …”; legacy ForC Succession names remain fallback defaults, existing scenarios unaffected.

title: landisutils 0.0.46
text: build_calibration_scenario_template() now resolves Dynamic Fire input filenames (InitialWeatherDatabase, Species_CSV_File, InitialFireEcoregionsMap, GroundSlopeFile, UphillSlopeAzimuthMap) template scenario’s dynamic-fire.txt directives, via new internal .calibration_directive_file() (also now backs .calibration_species_file()), instead assuming fixed names. Scenarios name weather DB initial-weather-database.csv Dynamic Fire species table dynamic-fire-species.csv (rather assumed initial_weather_database.csv DynamicFire_Spp_Table.csv) previously aborted calibration scenario reconstruction file.exists(...) failure add_file(); scenarios using previous names unaffected (remain fallback defaults).

title: landisutils 0.0.45
text: build_calibration_spinup_scenario() build_calibration_scenario_template() now resolve species-definitions file template scenario’s Species directive (new internal .calibration_species_file()) instead assuming name species.txt. Scenarios name species-core.txt (rather species.txt) previously aborted calibration spinup fs::file_exists(species_file) TRUE; scenarios use species.txt unaffected (remains fallback).

title: landisutils 0.0.44
text: save_observed_fire_targets() now derives fire_sizes_ha polygons’ SIZE_HA attribute polygons supplied non-empty, falling back points’ SIZE_HA otherwise. previous behaviour always read sizes points (NFDB agency-reported sizes), meant callers passing higher-quality perimeter polygons (e.g. NBAC’s ADJ_HA) primary_polys/secondary_polys got better data area_by_fuel_ha; size distribution still came NFDB. Now NBAC perimeters drive size CDF . Callers pass NFDB polygons see behaviour change NFDB polys carry SIZE_HA field. Callers without polys keep old points-driven behaviour. save_observed_fire_targets() primary_polys argument now optional (required). NULL, function falls back points-driven sizes skips area_by_fuel_ha computation. Matches existing optional treatment secondary_polys. Existing callers passing SpatVector unaffected.

title: landisutils 0.0.43
text: landis_run_docker() gains startup_jitter argument (reads LANDIS_STARTUP_JITTER environment variable NULL): set, call sleeps random runif(0, startup_jitter) seconds first touches Docker, staggering container launches large crew fleet overwhelm Docker daemon (stops answering docker stats returns exit 1 surge) hammer disk backing image layers renv library dozens replicates start . delay change results, tar_landis() bake targets command, tuning never invalidates completed replicates.

title: landisutils 0.0.42
text: landis_run_docker(), tar_landis(), landis_pool_start(), calibrate_dynamic_fire() config default cpu_limit change 4 2. Empirical measurement across 90 concurrent ForCS + Dynamic Fire + Dynamic Fuels containers live calibration shows LANDIS-II console process effectively single-threaded (median 1.00 cores, p99 1.11 cores, max 1.11 cores) – prior default 4 overprovisioned ~4x. new default 2 covers 99th-percentile .NET-GC / threadpool burst comfortable headroom letting users pack containers nominal CPU budget; 1 tight enough risk contention simulator thread .NET GC helper.

title: landisutils 0.0.41
text: Generated LANDIS-II input files longer embed timestamp >> generated landisutils header (package version kept). header now byte-reproducible, re-writing unchanged scenario produces identical files tar_landis()’s input-hash skip-check stays stable across Phase-3 rebuilds instead needlessly re-running every replicate.

title: landisutils 0.0.40
text: landis_archive_rep() new exported helper moves completed replicate directory scratch final location: fault-tolerant rsync ---partial (retry + linear backoff) copies sibling .partial staging dir destination filesystem, atomic rename publishes final dir ever appears complete, scratch source deleted afterwards (-op source destination resolve path). tar_landis() gains work_root argument: set (LANDIS_SCRATCH environment variable non-empty run time) replicate staged run fast, local, Docker-bind-mountable scratch finished rep moved final scenario_dir/repNN via landis_archive_rep(), value target returns – everything targets tracks – final location scratch holds transient run files; makes runs work scenario_dir lives storage Docker daemon bind-mount (e.g. root-squashed NFS share).

what: Fix: duplicate-dispatch corruption in LANDIS-II run helpers
title: landisutils 0.0.39
text: landis_run_docker() now derives container’s name deterministically scenario directory relies docker’s name uniqueness cross-worker mutex. replicate dispatched two workers (example targets re-runs branch false-positive worker crash original container still running), second call longer starts parallel container O_TRUNCs first run’s half-written outputs. Instead adopts -progress container, waits finish (applying post-completion watchdog orphan whose owning worker died hang), returns run’s result, duplicate dispatch reported success rather destroying replicate. landis_run_local() serializes replicate runs advisory filelock lock rep’s log/run.lock, preventing two concurrent dotnet processes corrupting directory. Adds filelock Imports.

what: Fix: corrupt BioSIM BUI values reaching downstream weather summaries
title: landisutils 0.0.38
text: get_fwi_daily() now recomputes BUI DMC/DC cffdrs, (input ) FWI recomputation. BioSIM FWI_Daily occasionally returns corrupt BUI (~5.8e5, DMC ~2.9e5); previously FFMC/ISI/FWI corrected, corrupt BUI propagated e.g. Dynamic Fire weather database (BUI column).

what: Fix: MinRelativeBiomass dropped its first ecoregion
title: landisutils 0.0.37
text: prepMinRelativeBiomass() now emits leading label column. insertMinRelativeBiomass() formats table .collapseRow(df, ) = df[, -1], drops first column (ShadeClass/label slot, since shade-class prefixes 1..5 hard-coded output). Without leading label column drop removed FIRST ECOREGION table header every shade-class row, Biomass Succession run aborted “Minimum relative biomass defined ecoregion 1” whenever active ecoregion set started lowest map code. Added prep -> insert regression test.

what: Per-trial loss-decomposition trace in Dynamic Fire calibration
title: landisutils 0.0.36
text: calibrate_dynamic_fire() now writes per-trial CSV alongside existing per-iter trace. new CSV (trial_trace_<timestamp>.csv out_dir) records, every objfn evaluation, parameter vector, total loss, raw per-component loss, weights, weighted per-component loss. new path returned trial_trace_path function’s return list. FORK workers write per-PID sidecars merged end, full evaluation history captured without locking. Useful plotting DEoptim trades four loss components (count, size, area_fuel, severity) converges – just per-iter best total. Falls back NA_character_ mock / parallel-disabled runs produce rows.

title: landisutils 0.0.35
text: prep_monthly_weather_biosim() gains ref_grid argument. supplied fixed reference grid (e.g. region-wide raster), monthly BioSIM pull cached grid’s STABLE GLOBAL cell ids one shared, accumulating store – overlapping / nested study areas reuse cells already fetched pull rest (run district, landscape unit within fetches nothing). Without ref_grid previous per-study-area (elevatr z) behaviour unchanged. create_locations_df() now reprojects grid cell coordinates lon/lat BioSIM, PROJECTED reference grid (e.g. aggregated rasterToMatch equal-area CRS) yields valid longDeg/latDeg; lon/lat grid (elevatr default) unaffected. store climate-(keyed CellID); ecoregion grouping applied assemble time: assemble_climate_library_file_monthly() gains cell_eco (CellID -> EcoID map) cell_ids (filter one study area’s cells). get_clim_monthly()’s public behaviour unchanged; BioSIM fetch factored internal helper shared new path. Internal: batch-x-year pull now uses purrr::map2() (sequential within call) rather furrr::future_map2() – orchestrator (targets/crew) owns cross-branch parallelism, internal furrr fan-parallel ambient plan overwhelmed shared BioSIM web service uninterruptible J4R socket hang.

what: landis_run_docker() no longer spawns a nested R session
title: landisutils 0.0.34
text: landis_run_docker() now launches docker run child via processx::process instead callr::r_bg(). old approach forked full background R session purely shell docker; ran crew/mirai worker, nested R process orphaned crash parent worker (start R ... crashed killed), aborted tar_make() SIGKILLed -flight LANDIS containers (exit 137, mid-run). processx simply exec()s docker CLI — lightweight child existing docker stats polls — eliminating interaction. Behaviour otherwise unchanged: docker run stays foreground (exit status container’s), --rm still auto-removes, post-completion watchdog resource logging identical, container exit code read via processx’s get_exit_status().

title: landisutils 0.0.33
text: calibrate_dynamic_fire() now forwards reltol steptol DEoptim::DEoptim.control(), project-friendly defaults reltol = 1e-3 (0.1% relative improvement) steptol = 25 generations. DEoptim halts itermax best--population objective fails improve reltol steptol consecutive generations. previous behaviour (run full itermax schedule regardless convergence) DEoptim default steptol = itermax; default left optimiser exposed long -improvement tails , project, intersected external Docker daemon restart aborted otherwise-converged run mid-trace. Callers can disable early stopping setting cfg$steptol >= cfg$itermax, restore upstream default setting cfg$steptol = NULL.

title: landisutils 0.0.32
text: landis_run_docker() now watches container’s stdout LANDIS-II console marker "Model run complete." SIGTERMs container fails exit within post_completion_timeout_sec seconds (default 300 = 5 min). long ForCS + Dynamic Fire scenarios many output extensions log completion marker spin .NET runtime shutdown path indefinitely (observed 25+ h hangs 100% CPU, outputs already disk). watchdog stops zombie containers treats exit codes 137/143 success completion marker seen, tar_make() correctly marks affected reps complete. sim outputs byte-identical clean exit watchdog fires LANDIS-II console reports completion. Pass post_completion_timeout_sec = Inf restore old (unbounded-wait) behaviour. tar_landis() gains matching post_completion_timeout_sec argument (default 300) forwards landis_run_docker(), watchdog grace period tunable (disablable Inf) targets pipeline. decision factored small internal predicate, .watchdog_should_stop(), unit coverage.

what: Biomass Succession support in the Dynamic Fire calibration setup
title: landisutils 0.0.31
text: build_calibration_spinup_scenario() build_calibration_scenario_template() now auto-detect succession backend (ForC Succession vs Biomass Succession) template directory wire appropriate succession extension, calibration freeze, output manifest. Previously hard-wired ForC Succession (required forc-succession.txt), Biomass Succession project build Dynamic Fire calibration scenario. Biomass Succession frozen calibration setting Timestep greater sim_years (SpinUp section, ForCS Soil.cs DOM-spinup workaround needed); ForCS behaviour unchanged.

title: landisutils 0.0.30
text: insertSeasonTable() now quantises ProportionFire column dyadic 1/128 fractions writing. Dynamic Fire System reads proportions single-precision floats rejects table (“Season Probabilities don’t add 1.0”) unless sum 1.0 essentially zero tolerance, arbitrary decimal proportions (e.g. observed fire counts divided total, sum 1.0 exact double-precision arithmetic) failed check unpredictably depending data. Dyadic 1/128 fractions exactly representable float, sum exactly 1.0 regardless summation order, round-trip table formatting; largest season absorbs rounding values still sum 1.0. Callers can now pass raw normalised season proportions rely table writer LANDIS-II compatibility (1/128 ~0.8% granularity).

title: landisutils 0.0.29
text: BiomassSuccession$new() longer force-writes SpinupCohorts SpinupMortalityFraction: keywords absent Core8 CoreV8.0-BiomassSuccession7.0 grammar, LANDIS-II v8 parser aborts (“Found name "SpinupCohorts" expected "MinRelativeBiomass"”) appear. now optional emitted set non-NULL value, default BiomassSuccession config parses runs stock landis-ii-v8-release image.

what: Request throttling + backoff for BioSIM weather fetches
title: landisutils 0.0.29
text: BioSIM weather retrieval now staggers requests retries exponential backoff. BioSIM web API can slow transiently unavailable load, BioSIM R client exposes timeout/retry knobs, get_clim_monthly(), get_clim_daily(), get_fwi_daily() route generateWeather() calls shared wrapper adds random pre-request delay, exponential-backoff retries, optional per-attempt timeout (resetting J4R client timeout). Behaviour tunable via landisutils.biosim.request_delay, landisutils.biosim.max_attempts, landisutils.biosim.backoff_base, landisutils.biosim.timeout options; set fetch process can see (e.g. worker-inherited .Rprofile).

what: Enable DOM spinup in calibration scenario template
title: landisutils 0.0.28
text: build_calibration_scenario_template() now patches calibration scenario’s forc-succession.txt SpinUp row 1  0  1  20 (DOM spinup , biomass spinup ), instead prior 0  0  1  20 (). Biomass spinup stays pre-calibration snapshot IC’s CohortBiomass values preserved verbatim, DOM spinup pass equilibrates ForCS’s soil-pool state via SpinupSoils(). Without , DisturbFireFromBiomassPools left partly-initialised state first cohort Dynamic Fire damages triggers NullReferenceException Extension-ForCS-Succession/src/Soil.cs:DisturbanceImpactsBiomass, aborting every calibration trial. Cost: ~30-60s extra startup per LANDIS-II trial.

title: landisutils 0.0.27
text: landis_pool_stop() now passes every container name single docker stop --time T <name1> <name2> ... single docker rm -f <name1> <name2> ..., instead looping one-container---time. Docker daemon parallelises stops internally, 90-container pool teardown drops ~15 minutes (90 x 10s SIGTERM deadlines, sequential) roughly timeout_sec wall time. function remains idempotent tolerant already-removed containers.

title: landisutils 0.0.26
text: warm Docker pool’s bind-mount source defaults <out_dir>/scratch, fails out_dir lives filesystem Docker daemon see (e.g. user-space autofs / sshfs / NFS mounts). daemon errors mkdir <mount-root>: permission denied resolving bind-mount path. cfg$scratch_root lets callers route pool’s scratch onto docker-visible local storage keeping out_dir project mount calibration trace CSV final outputs land alongside rest project.

title: landisutils 0.0.25
text: calibrate_dynamic_fire() now picks default cluster size parallelly::availableCores(constraints = "connections", omit = 2) parallelly installed, falling back parallel::detectCores() - 2L otherwise. detectCores() reports logical core count ignores R’s per-session connection cap (~125 default builds), large hosts (e.g. 256-core machines) naive default silently -provisions FORK cluster beyond R session can support. Explicitly setting cfg$n_cores short-circuits defaults. parallelly now Suggests.

title: landisutils 0.0.25
text: calibrate_dynamic_fire() longer passes parallelType = 1L DEoptim::DEoptim() supplying FORK cluster via control$cluster. DEoptim 2.2.8’s ctrl$cluster branch uses supplied cluster without binding local cl variable, post-loop cleanup unconditionally evaluates parallel::stopCluster(cl) whenever parallelType == "parallel", errors object 'cl' found. Leaving parallelType default (“none”) skips cleanup path still triggering parallel parApply(cl = ctrl$cluster, ...) evaluation DEoptim’s body. FORK cluster lifecycle fully managed calibrate_dynamic_fire() via .exit.

what: Clamp IgnProb to LANDIS-II’s permitted range
title: landisutils 0.0.25
text: patch_fire_config() apply_calibrated_ignprob() now clamp per-fuel IgnProb value [0, 1] applying calibration multiplier. Dynamic Fire System parser rejects IgnProb outside range Error input value Fuel type initiation probability: Value must 0 1.0 aborts run, previously caused every DEoptim trial whose multiplier pushed product 1.0 (e.g., IgnProb_Conifer = 1.5 default IgnProb = 1.0 Conifer surfaces C1-C5/C7/M1-M4) fail immediately. multiplier ranges smoke test calibration_par_names() unchanged; clamping just makes search-space boundary explicit instead relying every multiplier <= 1.0.

what: Retain failed-trial scratch directories for post-mortem
title: landisutils 0.0.25
text: sim_landis() now keeps failing trial’s per-rep scratch directory disk (prints path) LANDIS-II invocation errors. scratch previously deleted unconditionally .exit cleanup, made impossible inspect <trial_dir>/rep01/log/ underlying LANDIS-II stderr/stdout failed calibration trial. Successful trials still cleaned , adds disk usage failure. behaviour can still opted passing keep_scratch = TRUE (now retains dir success failure – prior keep_scratch = FALSE default still means “clean success ”).

title: landisutils 0.0.24
text: New overrides = list() argument lets callers substitute individual template files post-copy without touching production scenario. Useful cropping / aggregating specific inputs calibration (e.g., coarser fuel raster, smaller weather DB, substitute slope raster) without forking whole template. Accepted keys: ground_slope.tif, uphill_slope_azimuth.tif, fire-ecoregions.tif, initial_weather_database.csv, DynamicFire_Spp_Table.csv, species.txt, ecoregions.txt, ecoregions.tif, climate.txt. .tif overrides also carry .aux.xml / .tfw sidecars. Backward compatible: overrides = list() (default) preserves original “copy everything template_dir” behaviour.

title: landisutils 0.0.24
text: New exported landis_pool_restart_one(pool, idx) – stops + removes container index idx starts fresh replacement identical config (image, scratch_root bind-mount, user, cpu_limit, mem_limit) using new auto-generated container name. Pool state ($names[idx]) updated place also propagated caller’s frame loops can use current container name next iteration. landis_pool_exec() gains retries = 0L. > 0 exec command fails non-zero status, container restarted via landis_pool_restart_one() command retried, retries additional attempts. Useful long calibrations occasionally hit OOM kills daemon hiccups without wanting whole DEoptim run abort. Returns additional attempts field counting actual attempts (1 = retry needed; >1 = retries consumed). Pool object now carries start-time args (user_args, cpu_args, mem_args) restarts produce containers matching config. Refactor: container-creation logic moved internal .landis_pool_start_one() landis_pool_start() landis_pool_restart_one() share one code path.

title: landisutils 0.0.24
text: driver now runs battery cheap pre-flight checks function entry – starting warm Docker pool FORK cluster – catch common config / scenario / payload errors fast. Hard errors include: cfg$lower >= cfg$upper parameter (lists offending names). cfg$NP < 4 (DEoptim minimum), cfg$itermax < 1, cfg$n_reps < 1. cfg$weights zero (DEoptim nothing optimise). Unknown cfg$simulator (one "landis", "r_reimpl", "mock"). simulator = "landis": calibration scenario template missing LANDIS-II input files normally produced build_calibration_scenario_template(). Observed-targets payload missing required $primary shape. Docker available method = "docker"; LANDIS console findable method = "local". Scratch root writable. Soft signals (warnings / messages): NP < 10 * length(par_names) (DEoptim’s advisory, surfaced earlier). cfg$weights['area_fuel'] > 0 observed lacks area_by_fuel_ha / fuel_code_to_base; cfg$weights['severity'] > 0 observed lacks severity_dist. cases corresponding loss component contributes 0.

title: landisutils 0.0.24
text: New inst/scripts/calibration_smoke_test.R – complete 5-stage smoke test calibration plumbing (observed targets, spinup, scenario template, sim_landis trial, DEoptim loop) minimal scale (NP=4, itermax=2, n_reps=1, n_cores=2). Useful first-time setup, post- upgrade verification, confirming Docker + DEoptim installed correctly. Runs ~5-20 min via warm Docker pool. Usage:

title: landisutils 0.0.24
text: loss_from_stats() now computes L_area_fuel L_severity corresponding observed components present payload; previously stubbed zero. Components still gated weights entry, projects aren’t ready use stay Tier 1 (count + size) loss default. L_area_fuel chi-squared distance simulated observed burn-area--base-fuel-type proportions. Simulated area--fuel derived event’s init_fuel (ignition cell’s fuel code) times DamagedSites, mapped five base fuel types via observed$fuel_code_to_base. Activated observed$primary$area_by_fuel_ha observed$fuel_code_to_base set; contributes 0 otherwise. L_severity chi-squared distance simulated observed severity-class proportions. Simulated severities come event’s MeanSeverity (binned integer classes 1..5 half-integer boundaries); observed comes observed$primary$severity_dist, named numeric vector summing 1. Activated severity_dist non-NULL. save_observed_fire_targets() gains severity_dist = NULL argument callers can pass embed prior observed payload (e.g., new default_severity_prior_sturtevant2009()). New exported helper: default_severity_prior_sturtevant2009() returns named 5-element vector severity-class proportions derived Sturtevant et al. 2009. Intended starting point; projects override empirical priors available.

title: landisutils 0.0.24
text: new function family calibrating LANDIS-II Dynamic Fire System extension. calibration tunes SeverityCalibrationFactor, per-season FMC HiProp values, per-base-fuel-type IgnProb multipliers simulated fires match observed regional fire statistics; empirically-fit parameters (Mu, Sigma, Max, NumFires, seasonal PropFire) fit data layer upstream part optimisation. Pure-data helpers (LANDIS-II invocation): calibration_par_names() – canonical 9-name parameter vector. parse_dynamic_fire_logs() – reads fire/dynamic-fire-event-log.csv + fire/dynamic-fire-summary-log.csv small per-rep stats list. patch_fire_config() – surgical text patch dynamic-fire.txt (SeverityCalibrationFactor scalar; FireSizesTable HiProp columns; FuelTypeTable IgnProb column multiplied per base type). loss_from_stats() – multi-component weighted loss (count + KS--size Tier 1; area_by_fuel + severity stubbed weight 0 Tier 2). apply_calibrated_ignprob() / apply_calibrated_hi_prop() – table-level helpers used downstream calibration feed calibrated values production fire-config writers. Observed-target builder (one-time NFDB-derived summaries): save_observed_fire_targets() – writes small .rds per-ecoregion observed summaries. Project-agnostic: primary / secondary ecoregion SpatVectors + fuel_code_to_base mapping caller-provided. bc_fuel_code_to_base() – default fuel-code mapping BC FUEL_TYPE_CD factor encoding (codes 1..13); pass custom mapping project’s fuel-classification raster uses different encoding. Scenario builders static-landscape calibration scenario: build_calibration_spinup_scenario() – builds one-LANDIS-II scenario runs ForCS spinup flags emits snapshot spun-cohort community via Output Biomass Community extension. year-0 snapshot becomes calibration IC. run_calibration_spinup() – blocking single-trial LANDIS-II invocation spinup; dispatches landis_run_local() / landis_run_docker(). build_calibration_scenario_template() – copies production fire scenario, swaps IC spinup snapshot, patches ForCS calibration (spinup + frozen succession), optionally writes fresh baseline dynamic-fire.txt inline (breaks cycle calibrated_fire_params-aware production fire config calibration loop). write_landis_scenario_file() (scenarios.R) – lower-level scenario.txt writer takes already-written extension config-file paths rather R6 extension objects. Useful project pipelines write extension configs separate steps. Warm Docker pool calibration: landis_pool_start() / landis_pool_exec() / landis_pool_stop() – pool detached LANDIS-II containers docker execs per DEoptim trial instead docker run --rm per trial. Per-call env overrides (HOME=/tmp, DOTNET_BUNDLE_EXTRACT_BASE_DIR=...) keep dotnet accreting per-user-cache state trials. Designed .exit() teardown calibration drivers. Simulator backends calibration loop: sim_landis() – per-trial LANDIS-II invocation. Takes file paths (FORK-safe), copies template -> scratch dir, patches dynamic-fire.txt, runs LANDIS-II either via warm pool one-landis_run_docker() / landis_run_local(), parses logs. sim_r_reimpl() – reserved slot future pure-R reimplementation; currently errors -yet-implemented message. sim_mock() – plausibly-shaped output testing calibration driver’s control flow without Docker. DEoptim driver: calibrate_dynamic_fire() – orchestrates calibration: starts warm Docker pool, sets FORK cluster per-worker container pinning, invokes DEoptim::DEoptim() multi-component loss objective, tears pool + cluster via .exit(). Gated requireNamespace("DEoptim") (DEoptim Suggests; install via renv::install("DEoptim") calling). Vignette: vignette("Dynamic-Fire-Calibration", package = "landisutils") documents end--end target-wiring pattern downstream projects. Tests: 97+ testthat expectations across calibration + pool test files. Docker-gated tests skip_if_not() docker unavailable; DEoptim-gated driver tests skip_if_not_installed("DEoptim").

title: landisutils 0.0.23
text: cpu_limit = 4: maps docker run --cpus. LANDIS-II compute single-threaded (~1 core), .NET runtime spins 9-11 OS threads GC thread pool, 4 comfortable default. Pass NULL limit. mem_limit = "8g": baseline RAM cap (maps docker run --memory). Accepts numeric byte count string like "4g" / "512m". Pass NULL (mem_limit = Inf) limit. mem_margin = 1.5: headroom factor applied previously-observed peak (see auto-resolution ). Auto-resolution prior resource logs. running, function reads existing <rep_dir>/log/{docker,local}_resources.log rep. peak_mem_bytes * mem_margin exceeds baseline mem_limit, limit raised value rep ran fine last time never killed cap rerun. prior log exists rep (first run, rep dir freshly deleted), memory cap dropped entirely first run can discover needs; subsequent runs inherit empirically observed peak. CPU limit constant regardless history: LANDIS-II single-threaded .NET runtime doesn’t scale available cores.

what: Resource logs now self-describe the host
title: landisutils 0.0.22
text: landis_run_docker() landis_run_local() now append three additional lines rep’s docker_resources.log / local_resources.log: makes per-rep resource log self-describing: downstream provenance tooling can recover just rep used (elapsed_sec, peak_mem_bytes) host ran , important reps dispatched across heterogeneous cluster. New exported helpers: host_cpu_info() returns list(model, n_logical, ram_bytes), cross-platform: /proc/cpuinfo + /proc/meminfo Linux, sysctl machdep.cpu.brand_string + hw.memsize macOS, PROCESSOR_IDENTIFIER env var + wmic ComputerSystem ... TotalPhysicalMemory Windows. Logical-core count uses parallel::detectCores() everywhere. Called automatically run helpers. read_landis_resource_logs(run_dir) parses *_resources.log run_dir returns one data.frame row per rep fields. Used downstream report tooling summarise run-time / memory / host across replicates.
code: host_cpu_model: <model name>          # e.g. "AMD EPYC 7702 64-Core Processor" host_cpu_cores: <N>                   # logical cores visible to R host_ram_bytes: <N>                   # total system memory in bytes

title: landisutils 0.0.21
text: prepTopographyFile() (prepGroundSlopeFile() / prepUphillAzimuthMap() wrappers) now replaces NoData cells 0 writing INT2S raster. terra::terrain() leaves edge cells NaN (full 3x3 neighbourhood), becomes -32768 sentinel INT2S. LANDIS-II’s Dynamic Fire reader rejects active cell value: bug latent grids active cells, exposed whenever upstream changes (e.g. corrected non-veg masks) expand active area 1-cell edge band terrain raster. flat default (0 deg) safe: single-cell edge contributes negligibly Dynamic Fire’s rate--spread.

title: landisutils 0.0.20
text: skip check tar_landis() now input-aware. Previously run skipped whenever <rep_dir>/Landis-log.txt existed contained “Model run complete”, regardless whether inputs changed. meant targets correctly re-evaluated run target upstream input change (e.g. regenerated initial-communities.tif ecoregions.tif), surrounding command ran skip check still fired, landis_run_docker() / landis_run_local() never invoked rep dir kept stale outputs previous run. fix writes <rep_dir>/log/input_hash.json sidecar successful run, capturing SHA-1 (per-input-file MD5 + base_seed + rep_index + scenario_file). skip check now also requires saved hash match current input hash; mismatch triggers real rerun. New force = FALSE argument tar_landis(). Setting force = TRUE short-circuits skip check LANDIS-II always runs (useful debugging one-forced reruns without deleting rep dirs). Migration: existing rep dirs lack log/input_hash.json, first tar_make() upgrading rebuilds every rep. safe-conservative behaviour: can’t know whether existing outputs correspond current inputs. Users know rep current can sidestep rerun writing hash file manually.

title: landisutils 0.0.19
text: landis_run_docker() now writes <scenario_dir>/log/docker_image.log containing immutable sha256 digest image used run (<repo>@sha256:<64hex>, falling back local Id images registry origin). Image tags mutable; digest canonical identifier bytes actually ran. Downstream provenance tooling can read sidecar pin run specific image regardless subsequent tag movement. New pull = FALSE argument; TRUE, docker pull <image> runs simulation captured digest reflects current registry rather possibly-stale local copy. argument also exposed via tar_landis(pull = ...).

title: landisutils 0.0.18
text: leading_species() now returns "Non-vegetated" cells total biomass across species zero, instead falling alphabetical tiebreaker (arbitrarily assigned "Ac" defoliated cells). matches existing community_label() behaviour means transition / alluvial plots accurately reflect fire harvest impacts.

title: landisutils 0.0.17
text: Breaking API change: tar_landis() longer accepts n_reps. now takes explicit rep_index argument (unquoted upstream target symbol) returns single tar_target object (original API). caller creates rep-index target explicitly inside module list() alongside tar_landis() call. keeps targets visible tarborist’s static AST analysis (tarborist.additionalSingleTargetFactories handles run target; literal tar_target() call handles rep-index target): iteration = "vector" rep-index target enables cross() iterate individual elements, giving n_scenarios x n_reps independent branches dispatched crew workers parallel. branch runs one LANDIS-II simulation tracks replicate’s output files. Previously n_reps simulations ran inside single loop within one target branch. Caching scaling: adding replicates creates new branches – existing replicate results remain cached. Changing one replicate’s inputs invalidates branch. Previously change invalidated replicates.
code: list(   tar_target(name = ..._rep_index, command = seq_len(5L), iteration = "vector"),   landisutils::tar_landis(name = ..., rep_index = ..._rep_index, ...,                           pattern = cross(scenario_dir, ..._rep_index)) )

title: landisutils 0.0.17
text: landis_replicate() gains rep_index parameter single-replicate creation. Pass rep_index = (instead n_reps = N) create exactly one replicate directory (repNN/) without touching others. Useful replicate dispatched crew worker. n_reps now keyword-; positional second-argument form still works n_reps = clearer. function now requires exactly one n_reps rep_index. seed assigned rep_index used (base_seed + rep_index - 1) matches seed assigned n_reps mode index, results reproducible regardless call form used.

what: Vegetation dynamics: species biomass and transition plots
title: landisutils 0.0.16
text: New read_biomass_c_snapshots(paths, times, run_name) reads ForCS log_BiomassC.csv files (per-cohort, per-cell) one replicates, filtering requested snapshot years via arrow::open_dataset() lazy streaming multi-GB files never need fully materialised R. ForCS writes log_BiomassC.csv unconditionally, additional output extension required. New read_biomass_output_rasters(dirs, times, species, live_map_pattern, run_name) succession-agnostic alternative: reads per-species biomass rasters written Output.Biomass v4 extension (present landis-ii-v8-release Docker image). Works succession extension; requires Output.Biomass included scenario configuration. readers return identical data.table schema (scenario, replicate, Time, row, column, [ecoregion,] species, biomass Mg C ha^-1), downstream functions source-agnostic. New biomass_landscape_summary(df) aggregates per-cell snapshot data landscape-mean ± SD biomass species per timestep. New leading_species(df) labels cell snapshot species highest total live biomass. Ties broken alphabetically. New community_label(df, n_spp, min_pct) labels cell top-n species combination (e.g. "Hw-Sx"); species min_pct cell total dropped; zero-biomass cells labelled "Non-vegetated". New transition_data(label_df, times) builds lodes-form tibble required ggalluvial: unique label-path combinations across snapshot years, cell counts averaged across replicates. New plot_species_biomass(summary_df, colours, title) produces stacked area chart landscape-mean biomass species time. New plot_transitions(lodes_df, colours, title) produces Sankey-style alluvial diagram (via ggalluvial) showing cells move vegetation types across snapshot years. arrow, ggalluvial, ggplot2, purrr added Imports (previously absent Suggests).

title: landisutils 0.0.15
text: landis_run_docker(): container names now include calling process PID random integer suffix prevent name collisions multiple LANDIS replicates run simultaneously. tar_landis(): dependency file lists now deduplicated passed landis_replicate(). Paths normalised absolute form, filtered existing files, deduplicated basename; scenario-specific files (scenario directory) take priority cross-scenario duplicates. Applies Docker local run paths. tar_landis(): replicates LANDIS-II already completed successfully now skipped (idempotent re-run). Completion detected presence “Model run complete” Landis-log.txt. Applies Docker local run paths.

title: landisutils 0.0.14
text: insertDOMPools() now wraps multi-word pool names double-quotes, matching LANDIS-II parser’s requirement (e.g. "Fast AG" instead Fast AG). ForCS v4 changed four large parameter tables inline text CSV file references. insertEcoSppDOMParameters(), insertANPPTimeSeries(), insertMaxBiomassTimeSeries(), insertEstablishProbabilities() now write CSV path emit Keyword "filename" reference line, matching ForCS v4.0.2 input format. ForCS$write() passes self$path four CSV-writing insert*() functions registers resulting files via add_file() landis_replicate() copies replicate directory. four ForCS CSV filenames now prefixed ForCS_ (ForCS_EcoSppDOMParameters.csv, ForCS_ANPPTimeSeries.csv, ForCS_MaxBiomassTimeSeries.csv, ForCS_EstablishProbabilities.csv) origin unambiguous alongside extension files.

title: landisutils 0.0.14
text: LandisExtension subclasses produce fixed-name output files now expose output_files active binding listing files relative paths (e.g. log CSVs, summary CSVs). Extensions fixed outputs inherit base LandisExtension$output_files returns character(0). LandisScenario gains output_files active binding returns two LANDIS-II core outputs always written scenario directory: Landis-log.txt Metadata/LANDIS-II v8.0/LANDIS-II v8.0.xml. scenario() now writes output_manifest.txt scenario directory, listing fixed-name output files declared scenario extensions. manifest registered scenario$files landis_replicate() copies replicate directory. tar_landis() now reads output_manifest.txt base scenario directory includes listed files (absolute paths per replicate) returned character vector alongside log/ scan output_dir scan. ensures targets tracks log CSVs, Landis-log.txt, Metadata XML explicitly, without relying glob discovery.

title: landisutils 0.0.14
text: landis_run_docker() now tracks wall-clock elapsed time peak container memory. Docker launched via callr::r_bg() main thread can poll docker stats ---stream every 2 s; maximum observed RSS recorded peak memory. named container (e.g. landis-run-20260527123456) used stats lookup removed automatically --rm. Results printed completion written <scenario_dir>/log/docker_resources.log. --user uid:gid flag now skipped Windows (id -u/id -g available ; Docker Desktop Windows require ). landis_run_local() now tracks wall-clock elapsed time peak process memory. system2() replaced processx::process$new() (exposes subprocess PID handles working directory directly), main thread polls ps::ps_memory_info() every 2 s. ps package cross-platform, memory tracking works Linux, macOS, Windows without platform-specific shell commands. Results printed completion written <scenario_dir>/log/local_resources.log. functions now return named list (exit_code, elapsed_sec, peak_mem_bytes) instead bare integer exit code. processx ps added Imports (previously available transitive dependencies callr).

title: landisutils 0.0.13
text: DynamicFire$write() now calls add_file() InitialWeatherDatabase copied replicate directories landis_replicate(). Previously weather CSV silently absent, causing LANDIS-II fail runtime. tar_landis() output_dir now accepts character vector output subdirectory names. Pass c("output", "fire") using Dynamic Fire extension, writes maps event/summary logs fire/ subdirectory inside scenario directory.

title: landisutils 0.0.12
text: insertFile(), insertLandisData(), insertValue() now exported project-level code can build custom scenario files using primitives package uses internally. landis_find_docker() new helper returns path Landis.Console.dll inside container, reading getOption("landisutils.docker.console"). landis_run_docker() now calls instead duplicating lookup. landis_replicate() gains base_seed argument. set, RandomNumberSeed replicate’s scenario.txt rewritten base_seed + (rep_index - 1), giving every replicate distinct deterministic seed. Seeds index-stable: adding replicates later never changes seeds existing ones. tar_landis() gains base_seed argument, passed landis_replicate() baked command expression factory-call time crew workers receive correct value.

title: landisutils 0.0.11
text: Pin santoku GitHub source (hughjonesd/santoku) package archived CRAN 2026-05-15, broke zonal dependency resolution GitHub Actions CI.

title: landisutils 0.0.10
text: Remove unused PredictiveEcology packages: LandR, SpaDES.core, SpaDES.tools, reproducible, scfmutils, map – due broken dependency resolution.

title: landisutils 0.0.9
text: landis_run(): fix inverted stopifnot() guard — function previously rejected valid LandisScenario objects accepted everything else. New landis_run_docker() runs LANDIS-II simulation ephemeral Docker container (bind-mounting scenario directory /sim). New landis_run_local() runs LANDIS-II simulation directly via dotnet, writing stdout/stderr <scenario_dir>/log/. New tar_landis() factory creates targets format = "file" target runs LANDIS-II (locally via Docker) returns tracked output log files. New package options landisutils.docker.image landisutils.run.method set .onLoad().

title: landisutils 0.0.8
text: fix issue climr returning reference period rows - filter assembling data;

title: landisutils 0.0.7
text: prep_monthly_weather_climr() longer silently advertises "srad" supported variable. Vignette examples updated. get_elevation_rast() gained tmp_dir argument (default <landisutils.cache.path>/elevatr_tiles/) AWS Terrain Tile downloads land package cache instead leaking R session’s global tempdir() (elevatr’s default). climate test cleanup: new local_climate_test_cache() test helper (tests/testthat/helper-climate-cleanup.R) routes cache option, child-process TMPDIR, JVM java.io.tmpdir (used J4R J4RServer*.log hsperfdata_<user>/) per-test withr::local_tempdir(), tears future::plan(multisession) exit, BioSIM/climr/elevatr fetch tests longer accumulate /tmp residue across runs.

title: landisutils 0.0.6
text: added focused integration-test scenario necn_scrpple exercising NECNSuccession + SocialClimateFire plus biomass output extensions (OutputBiomass, OutputBiomassCommunity, OutputBiomassByAge, OutputBiomassReclass); validated end--end v8 Docker images; fixed OutputBiomassByAge$write() emitting one Species line per element (LANDIS-II parser accepts one Species keyword); species list now joined indented continuation lines format requires;

title: landisutils 0.0.5
text: daily monthly weather BioSIM via BioSIM package (prep_daily_weather(), prep_monthly_weather_biosim()); monthly weather climr (prep_monthly_weather_climr()), including bcgov-recommended 8-member GCM ensemble (climr_ensemble_8); monthly weather TerraClim via climateR (prep_monthly_weather()); exported lower-level fetch assembly helpers used (get_clim_daily(), get_clim_monthly(), get_clim_monthly_climr(), get_clim_monthly_terraclim(), get_fwi_daily(), get_elevation_rast(), create_locations_df(), assemble_climate_library_file*() family); climate caches now namespaced study-area hash distinct study areas don’t collide; added test_ecoregionPolys dataset support examples tests; reworked climate-data vignette demonstrate new backends; fixed BiomassSuccession R6 class name ("DynamicFuels"); added cffdrs digest Imports; added arrow, BioSIM, climr Suggests.

title: landisutils 0.0.4
text: improve docker integration tests;

title: landisutils 0.0.3
text: succession: DGS Succession, NECN Succession, PnET Succession; disturbance: Biomass Browse, Biomass Harvest, Climate BDA, EDA (Epidemiological Disturbance Agent), Forest Roads Simulation, Hurricane, Land Use Plus, Linear Wind, Magic Harvest, Original Wind, Root Rot; output: Output Biomass Community, Output Biomass-PnET, Output Biomass Reclass, Output Landscape Habitat, Output Local Habitat, Output Wildlife Habitat; added Multi-Extension-Scenarios vignette; miscellaneous updates fixes existing extensions.

title: landisutils 0.0.2
text: cache batch weather data acquisition year (#1); allow setting cache path using option landisutils.cache.path; change arguments start end prep_*_weather() integer years; use R6 classes track simulation input files; implemented scenario replication (#3); run LANDIS-II background process via callr;

title: landisutils 0.0.1
text: initial version;
